Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported from current statement of financial position date (rolling approach).

label.example: Lessee, Operating Lease, Liability, to be Paid, Year One

description.example: Amount of lessee's undiscounted obligation for lease payment for operating lease to be paid in next fiscal year following current fiscal year. Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported from current statement of financial position date (rolling approach).

label.example: Lessee, Operating Lease, Liability, to be Paid, Year Five

description.example: Amount of lessee's undiscounted obligation for lease payment for operating lease to be paid in fifth fiscal year following current fiscal year. Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported from current statement of financial position date (rolling approach).

label.example: Lessee, Operating Lease, Liability, to be Paid, Year Four

description.example: Amount of lessee's undiscounted obligation for lease payment for operating lease to be paid in fourth fiscal year following current fiscal year. Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported from current statement of financial position date (rolling approach).

label.example: Lessee, Operating Lease, Liability, to be Paid, Year Three

description.example: Amount of lessee's undiscounted obligation for lease payment for operating lease to be paid in third fiscal year following current fiscal year. Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported from current statement of financial position date (rolling approach).

label.example: Lessee, Operating Lease, Liability, to be Paid, Year Two

description.example: Amount of lessee's undiscounted obligation for lease payment for operating lease to be paid in second fiscal year following current fiscal year. Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported from current statement of financial position date (rolling approach).

label.example: Lessee, Operating Lease, Liability, to be Paid, Remainder of Fiscal Year

description.example: Amount of lessee's undiscounted obligation for lease payment for operating lease having initial or remaining lease term in excess of one year to be paid in remainder of current fiscal year.

label.example: Lessee, Operating Lease, Liability, Undiscounted Excess Amount

description.example: Amount of lessee's undiscounted obligation for lease payments in excess of discounted obligation for lease payments for operating lease.

label.example: Liabilities

description.example: Sum of the carrying amounts as of the balance sheet date of all liabilities that are recognized. Liabilities are probable future sacrifices of economic benefits arising from present obligations of an entity to transfer assets or provide services to other entities in the future.

label.example: Liabilities and Equity

description.example: Amount of liabilities and equity items, including the portion of equity attributable to noncontrolling interests, if any.

label.example: Liabilities, Current

description.example: Total obligations incurred as part of normal operations that are expected to be paid during the following twelve months or within one business cycle, if longer.

label.example: Liabilities, Noncurrent

description.example: Amount of obligation due after one year or beyond the normal operating cycle, if longer.

label.example: Long-term Debt

description.example: Amount, after unamortized (discount) premium and debt issuance costs, of long-term debt. Includes, but not limited to, notes payable, bonds payable, debentures, mortgage loans and commercial paper. Excludes capital lease obligations.

label.example: Long-term Debt, Current Maturities

description.example: Amount, after unamortized (discount) premium and debt issuance costs, of long-term debt, classified as current. Includes, but not limited to, notes payable, bonds payable, debentures, mortgage loans and commercial paper. Excludes capital lease obligations.

label.example: Long-Term Debt, Maturity, after Year Five

description.example: Amount of long-term debt payable, sinking fund requirement, and other securities issued that are redeemable by holder at fixed or determinable price and date, maturing after fifth fiscal year following current fiscal year. Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported from current statement of financial position date (rolling approach).

label.example: Long-Term Debt, Maturity, Year One

description.example: Amount of long-term debt payable, sinking fund requirement, and other securities issued that are redeemable by holder at fixed or determinable price and date, maturing in next fiscal year following current fiscal year. Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported from current statement of financial position date (rolling approach).

label.example: Long-Term Debt, Maturity, Year Five

description.example: Amount of long-term debt payable, sinking fund requirement, and other securities issued that are redeemable by holder at fixed or determinable price and date, maturing in fifth fiscal year following current fiscal year. Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported from current statement of financial position date (rolling approach).

label.example: Long-Term Debt, Maturity, Year Four

description.example: Amount of long-term debt payable, sinking fund requirement, and other securities issued that are redeemable by holder at fixed or determinable price and date, maturing in fourth fiscal year following current fiscal year. Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported from current statement of financial position date (rolling approach).

label.example: Long-Term Debt, Maturity, Year Three

description.example: Amount of long-term debt payable, sinking fund requirement, and other securities issued that are redeemable by holder at fixed or determinable price and date, maturing in third fiscal year following current fiscal year. Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported from current statement of financial position date (rolling approach).

label.example: Long-Term Debt, Maturity, Year Two

description.example: Amount of long-term debt payable, sinking fund requirement, and other securities issued that are redeemable by holder at fixed or determinable price and date, maturing in second fiscal year following current fiscal year. Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported from current statement of financial position date (rolling approach).

label.example: Long-Term Debt, Maturity, Remainder of Fiscal Year

description.example: Amount of long-term debt payable, sinking fund requirement, and other securities issued that are redeemable by holder at fixed or determinable price and date, maturing in remainder of current fiscal year.

label.example: Long-term Debt, Excluding Current Maturities

description.example: Amount after unamortized (discount) premium and debt issuance costs of long-term debt classified as noncurrent and excluding amounts to be repaid within one year or the normal operating cycle, if longer. Includes, but not limited to, notes payable, bonds payable, debentures, mortgage loans and commercial paper. Excludes capital lease obligations.

label.example: Long-term Purchase Commitment, Amount

description.example: The minimum amount the entity agreed to spend under the long-term purchase commitment.

label.example: Marketable Securities, Current

description.example: Amount of investment in marketable security, classified as current.

label.example: Marketable Securities, Noncurrent

description.example: Amount of investment in marketable security, classified as noncurrent.

label.example: Marketable Securities, Realized Gain (Loss)

description.example: Amount of realized gain (loss) on investment in marketable security, including other-than-temporary impairment (OTTI).

label.example: Marketing Expense

description.example: Expenditures for planning and executing the conception, pricing, promotion, and distribution of ideas, goods, and services. Costs of public relations and corporate promotions are typically considered to be marketing costs.

label.example: Net Cash Provided by (Used in) Financing Activities

description.example: Amount of cash inflow (outflow) from financing activities, including discontinued operations. Financing activity cash flows include obtaining resources from owners and providing them with a return on, and a return of, their investment; borrowing money and repaying amounts borrowed, or settling the obligation; and obtaining and paying for other resources obtained from creditors on long-term credit.

label.example: Net Cash Provided by (Used in) Financing Activities, Continuing Operations

description.example: Amount of cash inflow (outflow) of financing activities, excluding discontinued operations. Financing activity cash flows include obtaining resources from owners and providing them with a return on, and a return of, their investment; borrowing money and repaying amounts borrowed, or settling the obligation; and obtaining and paying for other resources obtained from creditors on long-term credit.

label.example: Net Cash Provided by (Used in) Investing Activities

description.example: Amount of cash inflow (outflow) from investing activities, including discontinued operations. Investing activity cash flows include making and collecting loans and acquiring and disposing of debt or equity instruments and property, plant, and equipment and other productive assets.

label.example: Net Cash Provided by (Used in) Investing Activities, Continuing Operations

description.example: Amount of cash inflow (outflow) of investing activities, excluding discontinued operations. Investing activity cash flows include making and collecting loans and acquiring and disposing of debt or equity instruments and property, plant, and equipment and other productive assets.

label.example: Net Cash Provided by (Used in) Operating Activities

description.example: Amount of cash inflow (outflow) from operating activities, including discontinued operations. Operating activity cash flows include transactions, adjustments, and changes in value not defined as investing or financing activities.

label.example: Net Cash Provided by (Used in) Operating Activities, Continuing Operations

description.example: Amount of cash inflow (outflow) from operating activities, excluding discontinued operations. Operating activity cash flows include transactions, adjustments, and changes in value not defined as investing or financing activities.

label.example: Net Income (Loss) Attributable to Parent

description.example: The portion of profit or loss for the period, net of income taxes, which is attributable to the parent.

label.example: Long-Lived Assets

description.example: Long-lived assets other than financial instruments, long-term customer relationships of a financial institution, mortgage and other servicing rights, deferred policy acquisition costs, and deferred tax assets.

label.example: Nonoperating Income (Expense)

description.example: The aggregate amount of income or expense from ancillary business-related activities (that is to say, excluding major activities considered part of the normal operations of the business).

label.example: Nontrade Receivables, Current

description.example: The sum of amounts currently receivable other than from customers. For classified balance sheets, represents the current amount receivable, that is amounts expected to be collected within one year or the normal operating cycle, if longer.

label.example: Notional Amount of Foreign Currency Derivative Instruments Not Designated as Hedging Instruments (Deprecated 2013-01-31)

description.example: Aggregate notional amount of all foreign currency derivatives not designated as hedging instruments. Notional amount refers to the number of currency units specified in the foreign currency derivative contract.

label.example: Number of Stores

description.example: Represents the number of stores.

label.example: OCI, before Reclassifications, before Tax, Attributable to Parent

description.example: Amount before tax and reclassification adjustments of other comprehensive income (loss) attributable to parent.

label.example: Operating Expenses

description.example: Generally recurring costs associated with normal operations except for the portion of these expenses which can be clearly related to production and included in cost of sales or services. Includes selling, general and administrative expense.

label.example: Operating Income (Loss)

description.example: The net result for the period of deducting operating expenses from operating revenues.

label.example: Operating Lease, Cost

description.example: Amount of single lease cost, calculated by allocation of remaining cost of lease over remaining lease term. Includes, but is not limited to, single lease cost, after impairment of right-of-use asset, calculated by amortization of remaining right-of-use asset and accretion of lease liability.

label.example: Operating Lease, Liability

description.example: Present value of lessee's discounted obligation for lease payments from operating lease.

label.example: Operating Lease, Liability, Current

description.example: Present value of lessee's discounted obligation for lease payments from operating lease, classified as current.

label.example: Operating Lease, Liability, Noncurrent

description.example: Present value of lessee's discounted obligation for lease payments from operating lease, classified as noncurrent.

label.example: Operating Lease, Payments

description.example: Amount of cash outflow from operating lease, excluding payments to bring another asset to condition and location necessary for its intended use.

label.example: Operating Lease, Right-of-Use Asset

description.example: Amount of lessee's right to use underlying asset under operating lease.

label.example: Operating Leases, Future Minimum Payments Due

description.example: Amount of required minimum rental payments for leases having an initial or remaining non-cancelable letter-terms in excess of one year.

label.example: Operating Leases, Future Minimum Payments Due, Next Twelve Months

description.example: Amount of required minimum rental payments for operating leases having an initial or remaining non-cancelable lease term in excess of one year due in the next fiscal year following the latest fiscal year. Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported on a rolling approach, from latest balance sheet date.

label.example: Operating Leases, Future Minimum Payments, Due in Five Years

description.example: Amount of required minimum rental payments for operating leases having an initial or remaining non-cancelable lease term in excess of one year due in the fifth fiscal year following the latest fiscal year. Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported on a rolling approach, from latest balance sheet date.

label.example: Operating Leases, Future Minimum Payments, Due in Four Years

description.example: Amount of required minimum rental payments for operating leases having an initial or remaining non-cancelable lease term in excess of one year due in the fourth fiscal year following the latest fiscal year. Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported on a rolling approach, from latest balance sheet date.

label.example: Operating Leases, Future Minimum Payments, Due in Three Years

description.example: Amount of required minimum rental payments for operating leases having an initial or remaining non-cancelable lease term in excess of one year due in the third fiscal year following the latest fiscal year. Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported on a rolling approach, from latest balance sheet date.

label.example: Operating Leases, Future Minimum Payments, Due in Two Years

description.example: Amount of required minimum rental payments for operating leases having an initial or remaining non-cancelable lease term in excess of one year due in the second fiscal year following the latest fiscal year. Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported on a rolling approach, from latest balance sheet date.

label.example: Operating Leases, Future Minimum Payments, Due Thereafter

description.example: Amount of required minimum rental payments for operating leases having an initial or remaining non-cancelable lease term in excess of one year due after the fifth fiscal year following the latest fiscal year. Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported on a rolling approach, from latest balance sheet date.

label.example: Operating Leases, Rent Expense, Net

description.example: Rental expense for the reporting period incurred under operating leases, including minimum and any contingent rent expense, net of related sublease income.

label.example: Other Accrued Liabilities, Current

description.example: Amount of expenses incurred but not yet paid classified as other, due within one year or the normal operating cycle, if longer.

label.example: Other Accrued Liabilities, Noncurrent

description.example: Amount of expenses incurred but not yet paid classified as other, due after one year or the normal operating cycle, if longer.

label.example: Other Assets, Current

description.example: Amount of current assets classified as other.

label.example: Other Assets, Noncurrent

description.example: Amount of noncurrent assets classified as other.

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Available-for-sale Securities Adjustment, Net of Tax (Deprecated 2012-01-31)

description.example: Gross appreciation or the gross loss in value of the total unsold securities. While for technical reasons this element has no balance attribute, the default assumption is a credit balance consistent with its label.

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Derivatives Qualifying as Hedges, Net of Tax (Deprecated 2012-01-31)

description.example: Net of tax effect change in accumulated gains and losses from derivative instruments designated and qualifying as the effective portion of cash flow hedges after taxes. A cash flow hedge is a hedge of the exposure to variability in the cash flows of a recognized asset or liability or a forecasted transaction that is attributable to a particular risk. The change includes an entity's share of an equity investee's increase or decrease in deferred hedging gains or losses. While for technical reasons this element has no balance attribute, the default assumption is a credit balance consistent with its label.

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Foreign Currency Transaction and Translation Adjustment, Net of Tax (Deprecated 2012-01-31)

description.example: Adjustment that results from the process of translating subsidiary financial statements and foreign equity investments into the reporting currency of the reporting entity, net of tax.

label.example: OCI, Debt Securities, Available-for-Sale, Gain (Loss), after Adjustment and Tax

description.example: Amount, after tax and adjustment, of unrealized gain (loss) on investment in debt security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in other comprehensive income (available-for-sale) and unrealized gain (loss) on investment in debt security measured at amortized cost (held-to-maturity) from transfer to available-for-sale.

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), before Reclassifications, before Tax

description.example: Amount before tax and reclassification adjustments of other comprehensive income (loss).

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), before Reclassifications, Net of Tax

description.example: Amount after tax, before reclassification adjustments of other comprehensive income (loss).

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Cash Flow Hedge, Gain (Loss), before Reclassification and Tax

description.example: Amount, before tax and reclassification, of gain (loss) from derivative instrument designated and qualifying cash flow hedge included in assessment of hedge effectiveness.

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Cash Flow Hedge, Gain (Loss), Reclassification, after Tax

description.example: Amount, after tax, of reclassification of gain (loss) from accumulated other comprehensive income (AOCI) for derivative instrument designated and qualifying as cash flow hedge included in assessment of hedge effectiveness.

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Cash Flow Hedge, Gain (Loss), Reclassification, before Tax

description.example: Amount, before tax, of reclassification of gain (loss) from accumulated other comprehensive income (AOCI) for derivative instrument designated and qualifying as cash flow hedge included in assessment of hedge effectiveness.

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Derivative, Excluded Component, Increase (Decrease), before Adjustments and Tax

description.example: Amount, before tax and adjustments, of gain (loss) from increase (decrease) in value of excluded component of derivative designated and qualifying as hedge. Adjustments include, but are not limited to, reclassifications for sale and settlement, and amounts recognized under systematic and rational method.

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Derivatives Qualifying as Hedges, Net of Tax

description.example: Amount after tax and reclassification adjustments, of increase (decrease) in accumulated gain (loss) from derivative instruments designated and qualifying as the effective portion of cash flow hedges and an entity's share of an equity investee's increase (decrease) in deferred hedging gain (loss).

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Foreign Currency Transaction and Translation Adjustment, Net of Tax

description.example: Amount after tax and reclassification adjustments of gain (loss) on foreign currency translation adjustments, foreign currency transactions designated and effective as economic hedges of a net investment in a foreign entity and intra-entity foreign currency transactions that are of a long-term-investment nature.

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Foreign Currency Translation Adjustment, Tax

description.example: Amount of tax expense (benefit), after reclassification adjustments of gain (loss) on foreign currency translation adjustments, foreign currency transactions designated and effective as economic hedges of a net investment in a foreign entity and intra-entity foreign currency transactions that are of a long-term-investment nature.

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Net of Tax

description.example: Amount after tax and reclassification adjustments of other comprehensive income (loss).

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Net of Tax, Portion Attributable to Parent

description.example: Amount after tax of other comprehensive income (loss) attributable to parent entity.

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Reclassification Adjustment for Sale of Securities Included in Net Income, Net of Tax (Deprecated 2013-01-31)

description.example: Net of tax amount of the income statement impact of the reclassification adjustment for unrealized gain (loss) realized upon the sale of available-for-sale securities.

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Reclassification Adjustment from AOCI for Sale of Securities, before Tax

description.example: Amount before tax of reclassification adjustment from accumulated other comprehensive income for unrealized gain (loss) realized upon the sale of available-for-sale securities.

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Reclassification Adjustment from AOCI for Sale of Securities, Net of Tax

description.example: Amount after tax of reclassification adjustment from accumulated other comprehensive income for unrealized gain (loss) realized upon the sale of available-for-sale securities.

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Reclassification Adjustment from AOCI for Sale of Securities, Tax

description.example: Amount of tax expense (benefit) of reclassification adjustment from accumulated other comprehensive income for unrealized gain (loss) realized upon the sale of available-for-sale securities.

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Reclassification Adjustment from AOCI on Derivatives, before Tax

description.example: Amount before tax of reclassification adjustment from accumulated other comprehensive income of accumulated gain (loss) realized from derivative instruments designated and qualifying as the effective portion of cash flow hedges and an entity's share of an equity investee's deferred hedging gain (loss).

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Reclassification Adjustment from AOCI on Derivatives, Net of Tax

description.example: Amount after tax of reclassification adjustment from accumulated other comprehensive income of accumulated gain (loss) realized from derivative instruments designated and qualifying as the effective portion of cash flow hedges and an entity's share of an equity investee's deferred hedging gain (loss).

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Reclassification Adjustment from AOCI on Derivatives, Tax

description.example: Amount of tax expense (benefit) of reclassification adjustment from accumulated other comprehensive income of accumulated gain (loss) realized from derivative instruments designated and qualifying as the effective portion of cash flow hedges and an entity's share of an equity investee's deferred hedging gain (loss).

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Reclassification Adjustment on Derivatives Included in Net Income, Net of Tax (Deprecated 2013-01-31)

description.example: Net of tax amount of the income statement impact of the reclassification adjustment of accumulated gain (loss) from derivative instruments designated and qualifying as the effective portion of cash flow hedges realized in net income. Also includes reclassification adjustments of an entity's share of an equity investee's deferred hedging gain (loss) realized in net income.

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Reclassification Adjustment on Derivatives Included in Net Income, Tax (Deprecated 2013-01-31)

description.example: Tax effect of the income statement impact of the reclassification adjustment of accumulated gain (loss) from derivative instruments designated and qualifying as the effective portion of cash flow hedges realized in net income. Also includes reclassification adjustments of an entity's share of an equity investee's deferred hedging gain (loss) realized in net income.

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Tax

description.example: Amount of tax expense (benefit) allocated to other comprehensive income (loss).

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Tax, Portion Attributable to Parent

description.example: Amount of tax expense (benefit) allocated to other comprehensive income (loss) attributable to parent entity.

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Reclassification Adjustment on Derivatives Included in Net Income, Net of Tax (Deprecated 2012-01-31)

description.example: Net of tax effect of the reclassification adjustment for accumulated gains and losses from derivative instrument designated and qualifying as the effective portion of cash flow hedges included in accumulated comprehensive income that was realized in net income during the period.

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Reclassification Adjustment on Derivatives Included in Net Income, Tax (Deprecated 2012-01-31)

description.example: Tax effect on reclassification adjustment for accumulated gains and losses from derivative instrument designated and qualifying as the effective portion of cash flow hedges included in accumulated comprehensive income that was realized in net income during the period.

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Unrealized Gain (Loss) on Derivatives Arising During Period, Net of Tax

description.example: Amount after tax of increase (decrease) in accumulated gain (loss) from derivative instruments designated and qualifying as the effective portion of cash flow hedges and an entity's share of an equity investee's increase (decrease) in deferred hedging gain (loss).

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Unrealized Gain (Loss) on Derivatives Arising During Period, Tax

description.example: Amount of tax expense (benefit), before reclassification adjustments, related to increase (decrease) in accumulated gain (loss) from derivative instruments designated and qualifying as the effective portion of cash flow hedges and an entity's share of an equity investee's increase (decrease) in deferred hedging gain (loss).

label.example: OCI, Debt Securities, Available-for-Sale, Unrealized Holding Gain (Loss), before Adjustment, after Tax

description.example: Amount, after tax and before adjustment, of unrealized holding gain (loss) on investment in debt security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in other comprehensive income (available-for-sale). Excludes unrealized gain (loss) on investment in debt security measured at amortized cost (held-to-maturity) from transfer to available-for-sale.

label.example: OCI, Debt Securities, Available-for-Sale, Unrealized Holding Gain (Loss), before Adjustment, Tax

description.example: Amount, before adjustment, of tax expense (benefit) for unrealized holding gain (loss) on investment in debt security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in other comprehensive income (available-for-sale). Excludes tax expense (benefit) for unrealized gain (loss) on investment in debt security measured at amortized cost (held-to-maturity) from transfer to available-for-sale.

label.example: Other Deferred Credits, Current

description.example: Amount of unearned revenue or income classified as other, expected to be taken into income within one year or the normal operating cycle, if longer.

label.example: Other Liabilities, Current

description.example: Amount of liabilities classified as other, due within one year or the normal operating cycle, if longer.

label.example: Other Liabilities, Noncurrent

description.example: Amount of liabilities classified as other, due after one year or the normal operating cycle, if longer.

label.example: Other Noncash Income (Expense)

description.example: Amount of income (expense) included in net income that results in no cash inflow (outflow), classified as other.

label.example: Other Nonoperating Income (Expense)

description.example: Amount of income (expense) related to nonoperating activities, classified as other.

label.example: Other Prepaid Expense, Current

description.example: Amount of asset related to consideration paid in advance for other costs that provide economic benefits within a future period of one year or the normal operating cycle, if longer.

label.example: Other Short-term Borrowings

description.example: Amount of borrowings classified as other, maturing within one year or the normal operating cycle, if longer.

label.example: Payments for (Proceeds from) Other Investing Activities

description.example: Amount of cash (inflow) outflow from investing activities classified as other.

label.example: Payments for Repurchase of Common Stock

description.example: The cash outflow to reacquire common stock during the period.

label.example: Payments of Dividends

description.example: Cash outflow in the form of capital distributions and dividends to common shareholders, preferred shareholders and noncontrolling interests.

label.example: Payments of Ordinary Dividends, Common Stock

description.example: Amount of cash outflow in the form of ordinary dividends to common shareholders of the parent entity.

label.example: Payment, Tax Withholding, Share-based Payment Arrangement

description.example: Amount of cash outflow to satisfy grantee's tax withholding obligation for award under share-based payment arrangement.

label.example: Payments to Acquire Available-for-sale Securities

description.example: The cash outflow to acquire debt and equity securities not classified as either held-to-maturity securities or trading securities which would be classified as available-for-sale securities and reported at fair value, with unrealized gains and losses excluded from earnings and reported in a separate component of shareholders' equity.

label.example: Payments to Acquire Debt Securities, Available-for-sale

description.example: Amount of cash outflow to acquire investment in debt security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in other comprehensive income (available-for-sale).

label.example: Payments to Acquire Businesses, Net of Cash Acquired

description.example: The cash outflow associated with the acquisition of a business, net of the cash acquired from the purchase.

label.example: Payments to Acquire Intangible Assets

description.example: The cash outflow to acquire asset without physical form usually arising from contractual or other legal rights, excluding goodwill.

label.example: Payments to Acquire Other Investments

description.example: Amount of cash outflow to acquire investments classified as other.

label.example: Payments to Acquire Productive Assets

description.example: The cash outflow for purchases of and capital improvements on property, plant and equipment (capital expenditures), software, and other intangible assets.

label.example: Payments to Acquire Property, Plant, and Equipment

description.example: The cash outflow associated with the acquisition of long-lived, physical assets that are used in the normal conduct of business to produce goods and services and not intended for resale; includes cash outflows to pay for construction of self-constructed assets.

label.example: Preferred Stock, Shares Authorized

description.example: The maximum number of nonredeemable preferred shares (or preferred stock redeemable solely at the option of the issuer) permitted to be issued by an entity's charter and bylaws.

label.example: Prepaid Expense Other, Noncurrent

description.example: Carrying amount as of the balance sheet date of amounts paid in advance which will be charged against earnings in periods after one year or beyond the operating cycle, if longer.

label.example: Proceeds from Issuance of Common Stock

description.example: The cash inflow from the additional capital contribution to the entity.

label.example: Proceeds from Issuance of Long-term Debt

description.example: The cash inflow from a debt initially having maturity due after one year or beyond the operating cycle, if longer.

label.example: Proceeds from Maturities, Prepayments and Calls of Debt Securities, Available-for-sale

description.example: Amount of cash inflow from maturity, prepayment and call of investment in debt security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in other comprehensive income (available-for-sale).

label.example: Proceeds from Other Short-term Debt

description.example: Amount of cash inflow from short-term debt classified as other.

label.example: Proceeds from (Payments for) Other Financing Activities

description.example: Amount of cash inflow (outflow) from financing activities classified as other.

label.example: Proceeds from (Repayments of) Commercial Paper

description.example: The net cash inflow or cash outflow from issuing (borrowing) and repaying commercial paper.

label.example: Proceeds from (Repayments of) Short-term Debt

description.example: The net cash inflow or outflow for borrowing having initial term of repayment within one year or the normal operating cycle, if longer.

label.example: Proceeds from (Repayments of) Short-term Debt, Maturing in More than Three Months

description.example: The cash inflow from a borrowing net of the cash outflow from repayment of a borrowing having initial term of repayment of more than three months but less than one year or one operating cycle (if the normal cycle is more than one year).

label.example: Proceeds from (Repayments of) Short-term Debt, Maturing in Three Months or Less

description.example: The cash inflow from a borrowing net of the cash outflow from repayment of a borrowing having initial term of repayment within three months.

label.example: Proceeds from Sale and Maturity of Other Investments

description.example: The cash inflow associated with the sale and maturity (principal being due) of other investments, prepayment and call (request of early payment) of other investments not otherwise defined in the taxonomy.

label.example: Proceeds from Sale of Available-for-sale Securities

description.example: The cash inflow associated with the sale of debt and equity securities classified as available-for-sale securities.

label.example: Proceeds from Sale of Debt Securities, Available-for-sale

description.example: Amount of cash inflow from sale of investment in debt security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in other comprehensive income (available-for-sale).

label.example: Proceeds from Short-term Debt, Maturing in More than Three Months

description.example: The cash inflow from a borrowing having initial term of repayment of more than three months but less than one year or one operating cycle (if the normal cycle is more than one year).

label.example: Product Warranty Accrual, Current

description.example: Carrying value as of the balance sheet date of obligations incurred through that date and payable for estimated claims under standard and extended warranty protection rights granted to customers. For classified balance sheets, represents the current portion of the liabilities (due within one year or within the normal operating cycle if longer).

label.example: Standard and Extended Product Warranty Accrual, Decrease for Payments

description.example: Amount of decrease in the standard and extended product warranty accrual from payments made in cash or in kind to satisfy claims under the terms of the standard and extended product warranty.

label.example: Standard and Extended Product Warranty Accrual, Increase for Warranties Issued

description.example: Amount of increase in the standard and extended product warranty accrual from warranties issued.

label.example: Product Warranty Expense

description.example: The expense charged against earnings for the period pertaining to standard and extended warranties on the entity's goods and services granted to customers.

label.example: Property, Plant and Equipment, Gross

description.example: Amount before accumulated depreciation, depletion and amortization of physical assets used in the normal conduct of business and not intended for resale. Examples include, but are not limited to, land, buildings, machinery and equipment, office equipment, and furniture and fixtures.

label.example: Property, Plant and Equipment, Net

description.example: Amount after accumulated depreciation, depletion and amortization of physical assets used in the normal conduct of business to produce goods and services and not intended for resale. Examples include, but are not limited to, land, buildings, machinery and equipment, office equipment, and furniture and fixtures.

label.example: Reclassification from Accumulated Other Comprehensive Income, Current Period, before Tax

description.example: Amount before tax of reclassification adjustments of other comprehensive income (loss).

label.example: Reclassification from Accumulated Other Comprehensive Income, Current Period, Net of Tax

description.example: Amount after tax of reclassification adjustments of other comprehensive income (loss).

label.example: Reclassification from AOCI, Current Period, before Tax, Attributable to Parent

description.example: Amount before tax of reclassification adjustments of other comprehensive income (loss) attributable to parent.

label.example: Repayments of Long-term Debt

description.example: The cash outflow for debt initially having maturity due after one year or beyond the normal operating cycle, if longer.

label.example: Repayments of Other Short-term Debt

description.example: Amount of cash outflow for the payment of debt classified as other, maturing within one year or the operating cycle, if longer.

label.example: Repayments of Short-term Debt, Maturing in More than Three Months

description.example: The cash outflow from a repayment of a borrowing having initial term of repayment of more than three months but less than one year or one operating cycle (if the normal cycle is more than one year).

label.example: Research and Development Expense

description.example: The aggregate costs incurred (1) in a planned search or critical investigation aimed at discovery of new knowledge with the hope that such knowledge will be useful in developing a new product or service, a new process or technique, or in bringing about a significant improvement to an existing product or process; or (2) to translate research findings or other knowledge into a plan or design for a new product or process or for a significant improvement to an existing product or process whether intended for sale or the entity's use, during the reporting period charged to research and development projects, including the costs of developing computer software up to the point in time of achieving technological feasibility, and costs allocated in accounting for a business combination to in-process projects deemed to have no alternative future use.

label.example: Restricted Cash

description.example: Amount of cash restricted as to withdrawal or usage. Cash includes, but is not limited to, currency on hand, demand deposits with banks or financial institutions, and other accounts with general characteristics of demand deposits.

label.example: Restricted Cash and Cash Equivalents, Current

description.example: Amount of cash and cash equivalents restricted as to withdrawal or usage, classified as current. Cash includes, but is not limited to, currency on hand, demand deposits with banks or financial institutions, and other accounts with general characteristics of demand deposits. Cash equivalents include, but are not limited to, short-term, highly liquid investments that are both readily convertible to known amounts of cash and so near their maturity that they present insignificant risk of changes in value because of changes in interest rates.

label.example: Restricted Cash and Cash Equivalents, Noncurrent

description.example: Amount of cash and cash equivalents restricted as to withdrawal or usage, classified as noncurrent. Cash includes, but is not limited to, currency on hand, demand deposits with banks or financial institutions, and other accounts with general characteristics of demand deposits. Cash equivalents include, but are not limited to, short-term, highly liquid investments that are both readily convertible to known amounts of cash and so near their maturity that they present insignificant risk of changes in value because of changes in interest rates.

label.example: Restricted Investments

description.example: Investments which are not defined as or included in marketable (debt, equity, or other) securities whose use is restricted in whole or in part, generally by contractual agreements or regulatory requirements. For use in an unclassified balance sheet.

label.example: Retained Earnings (Accumulated Deficit)

description.example: The cumulative amount of the reporting entity's undistributed earnings or deficit.

label.example: Revenue from Contract with Customer, Excluding Assessed Tax

description.example: Amount, excluding tax collected from customer, of revenue from satisfaction of performance obligation by transferring promised good or service to customer. Tax collected from customer is tax assessed by governmental authority that is both imposed on and concurrent with specific revenue-producing transaction, including, but not limited to, sales, use, value added and excise.

label.example: Revenues

description.example: Amount of revenue recognized from goods sold, services rendered, insurance premiums, or other activities that constitute an earning process. Includes, but is not limited to, investment and interest income before deduction of interest expense when recognized as a component of revenue, and sales and trading gain (loss).

label.example: Revenue, Net (Deprecated 2018-01-31)

description.example: Total revenue from sale of goods and services rendered during the reporting period, in the normal course of business, reduced by sales returns and allowances, and sales discounts.

label.example: Sales Revenue, Services, Other (Deprecated 2018-01-31)

description.example: Amount before allowances and discounts of service revenue classified as other.

label.example: Securities Sold under Agreements to Repurchase, Fair Value of Collateral

description.example: Fair value of securities pledged as collateral against securities sold under agreement to repurchase.

label.example: Segment Reporting Information, Operating Income (Loss) (Deprecated 2011-01-31)

description.example: Amount of income or loss for the reportable segment before unusual Items, equity method income or loss, income taxes, and extraordinary items.

label.example: Segment Reporting, Reconciling Items, Assets (Deprecated 2011-01-31)

description.example: Total of reconciling items between total reportable segments' assets and consolidated entity assets.

label.example: Segment Reporting, Segment Assets (Deprecated 2011-01-31)

description.example: Total assets for reportable segments.

label.example: Selling, General and Administrative Expense

description.example: The aggregate total costs related to selling a firm's product and services, as well as all other general and administrative expenses. Direct selling expenses (for example, credit, warranty, and advertising) are expenses that can be directly linked to the sale of specific products. Indirect selling expenses are expenses that cannot be directly linked to the sale of specific products, for example telephone expenses, Internet, and postal charges. General and administrative expenses include salaries of non-sales personnel, rent, utilities, communication, etc.

label.example: Share-based Payment Arrangement, Noncash Expense

description.example: Amount of noncash expense for share-based payment arrangement.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Equity Instruments Other than Options, Forfeited in Period

description.example: The number of equity-based payment instruments, excluding stock (or unit) options, that were forfeited during the reporting period.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Equity Instruments Other than Options, Forfeited in Period, Weighted Average Grant Date Fair Value (Deprecated 2012-01-31)

description.example: The weighted average fair value as of the grant date of equity-based award plans other than stock (unit) option plans that were not exercised or put into effect during the reporting period as a result of the occurrence of a terminating event specified in the contractual agreement of the plan.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Equity Instruments Other than Options, Forfeitures, Weighted Average Grant Date Fair Value

description.example: Weighted average fair value as of the grant date of equity-based award plans other than stock (unit) option plans that were not exercised or put into effect as a result of the occurrence of a terminating event.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Equity Instruments Other than Options, Grants in Period

description.example: The number of grants made during the period on other than stock (or unit) option plans (for example, phantom stock or unit plan, stock or unit appreciation rights plan, performance target plan).

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Equity Instruments Other than Options, Grants in Period, Weighted Average Grant Date Fair Value

description.example: The weighted average fair value at grant date for nonvested equity-based awards issued during the period on other than stock (or unit) option plans (for example, phantom stock or unit plan, stock or unit appreciation rights plan, performance target plan).

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Equity Instrument Other than Option, Nonvested, Intrinsic Value

description.example: Per share or unit weighted-average intrinsic value of nonvested award under share-based payment arrangement. Excludes share and unit options.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Equity Instruments Other than Options, Nonvested, Number

description.example: The number of non-vested equity-based payment instruments, excluding stock (or unit) options, that validly exist and are outstanding as of the balance sheet date.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Equity Instruments Other than Options, Nonvested, Weighted Average Grant Date Fair Value

description.example: Per share or unit weighted-average fair value of nonvested award under share-based payment arrangement. Excludes share and unit options.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Equity Instruments Other than Options, Vested in Period

description.example: The number of equity-based payment instruments, excluding stock (or unit) options, that vested during the reporting period.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Equity Instruments Other than Options, Vested in Period, Fair Value

description.example: Fair value of share-based awards for which the grantee gained the right by satisfying service and performance requirements, to receive or retain shares or units, other instruments, or cash.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Equity Instruments Other than Options, Vested in Period, Weighted Average Grant Date Fair Value

description.example: The weighted average fair value as of grant date pertaining to an equity-based award plan other than a stock (or unit) option plan for which the grantee gained the right during the reporting period, by satisfying service and performance requirements, to receive or retain shares or units, other instruments, or cash in accordance with the terms of the arrangement.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Fair Value Assumptions, Expected Dividend Rate

description.example: The estimated dividend rate (a percentage of the share price) to be paid (expected dividends) to holders of the underlying shares over the option's term.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Fair Value Assumptions, Expected Term (Deprecated 2012-01-31)

description.example: The period of time an equity-based award is expected to be outstanding. An equity-based award's expected term is generally determined based on, among other factors, the instrument's contractual term and the effects of employees' expected exercise and post-vesting employment termination behavior.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Fair Value Assumptions, Risk Free Interest Rate

description.example: The risk-free interest rate assumption that is used in valuing an option on its own shares.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Fair Value Assumptions, Weighted Average Volatility Rate

description.example: Rate of weighted-average expected volatility for award under share-based payment arrangement.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Number of Shares Available for Grant

description.example: The difference between the maximum number of shares (or other type of equity) authorized for issuance under the plan (including the effects of amendments and adjustments), and the sum of: 1) the number of shares (or other type of equity) already issued upon exercise of options or other equity-based awards under the plan; and 2) shares (or other type of equity) reserved for issuance on granting of outstanding awards, net of cancellations and forfeitures, if applicable.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Exercisable, Intrinsic Value (Deprecated 2011-01-31)

description.example: The total dollar difference between fair values of the underlying shares reserved for issuance and exercise prices of vested portions of options outstanding and currently exercisable under the option plan as of the balance sheet date.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Exercisable, Number

description.example: The number of shares into which fully or partially vested stock options outstanding as of the balance sheet date can be currently converted under the option plan.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Exercisable, Weighted Average Exercise Price

description.example: The weighted-average price as of the balance sheet date at which grantees can acquire the shares reserved for issuance on vested portions of options outstanding and currently exercisable under the stock option plan.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Exercisable, Weighted Average Remaining Contractual Term (Deprecated 2012-01-31)

description.example: The weighted average period between the balance sheet date and expiration for all vested portions of options outstanding and currently exercisable (or convertible) under the plan, which may be expressed in a decimal value for number of years.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Exercises in Period (Deprecated 2011-01-31)

description.example: The decrease in the number of reserved shares that could potentially be issued attributable to the exercise or conversion during the reporting period of previously issued stock options under the option plan.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Exercises in Period, Intrinsic Value

description.example: Amount of accumulated difference between fair value of underlying shares on dates of exercise and exercise price on options exercised (or share units converted) into shares.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Exercises in Period, Weighted Average Exercise Price (Deprecated 2012-01-31)

description.example: The weighted average price at which option holders acquired shares when converting their stock options into shares under the plan during the reporting period.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Forfeitures in Period

description.example: The number of shares under options that were cancelled during the reporting period as a result of occurrence of a terminating event specified in contractual agreements pertaining to the stock option plan.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Forfeitures in Period, Weighted Average Exercise Price (Deprecated 2012-01-31)

description.example: The weighted average price at which grantees could have acquired the underlying shares with respect to stock options that were terminated during the reporting period due to noncompliance with plan terms during the reporting period.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Grants in Period, Net of Forfeitures

description.example: Net number of share options (or share units) granted during the period.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Grants in Period, Weighted Average Exercise Price (Deprecated 2012-01-31)

description.example: The weighted average price as of the balance sheet date at which grantees can acquire the shares reserved for issuance on stock options awarded under the plan during the reporting period.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Grants in Period, Weighted Average Grant Date Fair Value

description.example: The weighted average grant-date fair value of options granted during the reporting period as calculated by applying the disclosed option pricing methodology.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Other Increases (Decreases) in Period

description.example: The addition or reduction in the number of reserved shares that could potentially be issued under the option plan attributable to reasons other than grants, exercises, forfeitures, and expirations during the reporting period.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Outstanding, Intrinsic Value

description.example: Amount by which the current fair value of the underlying stock exceeds the exercise price of options outstanding.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Outstanding, Number

description.example: Number of options outstanding, including both vested and non-vested options.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Outstanding, Weighted Average Exercise Price

description.example: Weighted average price at which grantees can acquire the shares reserved for issuance under the stock option plan.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Outstanding, Weighted Average Remaining Contractual Term (Deprecated 2011-01-31)

description.example: The weighted average period between the balance sheet date and expiration for all awards outstanding under the plan, which may be expressed in a decimal value for number of years.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Vested and Expected to Vest, Outstanding, Number

description.example: Number of fully vested and expected to vest options outstanding that can be converted into shares under option plan. Includes, but is not limited to, unvested options for which requisite service period has not been rendered but that are expected to vest based on achievement of performance condition, if forfeitures are recognized when they occur.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangements by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Exercises in Period, Weighted Average Exercise Price

description.example: Weighted average price at which option holders acquired shares when converting their stock options into shares.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangements by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Forfeitures in Period, Weighted Average Exercise Price

description.example: Weighted average price at which grantees could have acquired the underlying shares with respect to stock options that were terminated.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangements by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Grants in Period, Weighted Average Exercise Price

description.example: Weighted average per share amount at which grantees can acquire shares of common stock by exercise of options.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Equity Instruments Other than Options, Aggregate Intrinsic Value, Nonvested

description.example: Intrinsic value of nonvested award under share-based payment arrangement. Excludes share and unit options.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Exercisable, Intrinsic Value

description.example: Amount of difference between fair value of the underlying shares reserved for issuance and exercise price of vested portions of options outstanding and currently exercisable.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Outstanding, Weighted Average Remaining Contractual Term (Deprecated 2012-01-31)

description.example: The weighted average period between the balance sheet date and expiration for all awards outstanding under the plan, which may be expressed in a decimal value for number of years.

label.example: Share-based Payment Arrangement, Shares Withheld for Tax Withholding Obligation

description.example: Number of shares used to settle grantee's tax withholding obligation for award under share-based payment arrangement.

label.example: Short-term Debt, Weighted Average Interest Rate, at Point in Time

description.example: Weighted average interest rate of short-term debt outstanding calculated at point in time.

label.example: Significant (Increase) Decrease in Unrecognized Tax Benefits is Reasonably Possible, Estimated Range of Change, Lower Bound (Deprecated 2015-01-31)

description.example: Amount of (increase) decrease reasonably possible in the estimated lower bound of the unrecognized tax benefit for a tax position taken or expected to be taken.

label.example: Significant (Increase) Decrease in Unrecognized Tax Benefits is Reasonably Possible, Estimated Range of Change, Upper Bound (Deprecated 2015-01-31)

description.example: Amount of (increase) decrease reasonably possible in the estimated upper bound of the unrecognized tax benefit for a tax position taken or expected to be taken.

label.example: Standard Product Warranty Accrual

description.example: Amount as of the balance sheet date of the aggregate standard product warranty liability. Does not include the balance for the extended product warranty liability.

label.example: Standard Product Warranty Accrual, Decrease for Payments

description.example: Amount of decrease in the standard product warranty accrual from payments made in cash or in kind to satisfy claims under the terms of the standard product warranty. Excludes extended product warranties.

label.example: Standard Product Warranty Accrual, Increase for Warranties Issued

description.example: Amount of increase in the standard product warranty accrual from warranties issued. Excludes extended product warranties.

label.example: State and Local Income Tax Expense (Benefit), Continuing Operations

description.example: Amount of state and local current and deferred income tax expense (benefit) attributable to continuing operations.

label.example: Stockholders' Equity Attributable to Parent

description.example: Total of all stockholders' equity (deficit) items, net of receivables from officers, directors, owners, and affiliates of the entity which are attributable to the parent. The amount of the economic entity's stockholders' equity attributable to the parent excludes the amount of stockholders' equity which is allocable to that ownership interest in subsidiary equity which is not attributable to the parent (noncontrolling interest, minority interest). This excludes temporary equity and is sometimes called permanent equity.

label.example: Stockholders' Equity Note, Stock Split, Conversion Ratio

description.example: Ratio applied to the conversion of stock split, for example but not limited to, one share converted to two or two shares converted to one.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Exercises in Period

description.example: Number of share options (or share units) exercised during the current period.

label.example: Stock Issued During Period, Value, Acquisitions

description.example: Value of stock issued pursuant to acquisitions during the period.

label.example: Shares Issued, Value, Share-based Payment Arrangement, after Forfeiture

description.example: Value, after forfeiture, of shares issued under share-based payment arrangement. Excludes employee stock ownership plan (ESOP).

label.example: Stock Repurchased and Retired During Period, Shares

description.example: Number of shares that have been repurchased and retired during the period.

label.example: Stock Repurchased and Retired During Period, Value

description.example: Equity impact of the value of stock that has been repurchased and retired during the period. The excess of the purchase price over par value can be charged against retained earnings (once the excess is fully allocated to additional paid in capital).

label.example: Stock Repurchase Program, Authorized Amount (Deprecated 2014-01-31)

description.example: Amount of a stock repurchase plan authorized by an entity's Board of Directors.

label.example: Stock Repurchase Program, Authorized Amount

description.example: Amount of stock repurchase plan authorized.

label.example: Tax Adjustments, Settlements, and Unusual Provisions

description.example: Amount of increase (decrease) to previously recorded tax expense. Includes, but is not limited to, significant settlements of income tax disputes, and unusual tax positions or infrequent actions taken by the entity, including tax assessment reversal, and IRS tax settlement.

label.example: Taxes Payable, Current

description.example: Carrying value as of the balance sheet date of obligations incurred and payable for statutory income, sales, use, payroll, excise, real, property and other taxes. Used to reflect the current portion of the liabilities (due within one year or within the normal operating cycle if longer).

label.example: Derivatives used in Net Investment Hedge, Net of Tax

description.example: Amount, after tax, of cumulative translation adjustment attributable to gain (loss) on foreign currency derivatives that are designated as, and are effective as, economic hedges of a net investment in a foreign entity.

label.example: Treasury Stock, Value, Acquired, Cost Method

description.example: Equity impact of the cost of common and preferred stock that were repurchased during the period. Recorded using the cost method.

label.example: Undistributed Earnings of Foreign Subsidiaries

description.example: Amount of undistributed earnings of foreign subsidiaries intended to be permanently reinvested outside the country of domicile.

label.example: Unrecognized Tax Benefits

description.example: Amount of unrecognized tax benefits.

label.example: Unrecognized Tax Benefits, Decrease Resulting from Prior Period Tax Positions

description.example: Amount of decrease in unrecognized tax benefits resulting from tax positions taken in prior period tax returns.

label.example: Unrecognized Tax Benefits, Decrease Resulting from Settlements with Taxing Authorities

description.example: Amount of decrease in unrecognized tax benefits resulting from settlements with taxing authorities.

label.example: Unrecognized Tax Benefits, Income Tax Penalties and Interest Accrued

description.example: Amount accrued for interest on an underpayment of income taxes and penalties related to a tax position claimed or expected to be claimed in the tax return.

label.example: Unrecognized Tax Benefits, Income Tax Penalties and Interest Expense

description.example: Amount of expense for interest on an underpayment of income taxes and penalties related to a tax position claimed or expected to be claimed in the tax return.

label.example: Unrecognized Tax Benefits, Increase Resulting from Current Period Tax Positions

description.example: Amount of increase in unrecognized tax benefits resulting from tax positions that have been or will be taken in current period tax return.

label.example: Unrecognized Tax Benefits, Increase Resulting from Prior Period Tax Positions

description.example: Amount of increase in unrecognized tax benefits resulting from tax positions taken in prior period tax returns.

label.example: Unrecognized Tax Benefits, Period Increase (Decrease)

description.example: Amount of increase (decrease) in unrecognized tax benefits attributable to uncertain tax positions taken in tax returns.

label.example: Unrecognized Tax Benefits, Reduction Resulting from Lapse of Applicable Statute of Limitations

description.example: Amount of decrease in unrecognized tax benefits resulting from lapses of applicable statutes of limitations.

label.example: Unrecognized Tax Benefits that Would Impact Effective Tax Rate

description.example: The total amount of unrecognized tax benefits that, if recognized, would affect the effective tax rate.

label.example: Unrecorded Unconditional Purchase Obligation, to be Paid, Year Five

description.example: Amount of fixed and determinable portion of unrecorded unconditional purchase obligation to be paid in fifth fiscal year following current fiscal year. Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported from current statement of financial position date (rolling approach).

label.example: Unrecorded Unconditional Purchase Obligation, to be Paid, Year One

description.example: Amount of fixed and determinable portion of unrecorded unconditional purchase obligation to be paid in next fiscal year following current fiscal year. Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported from current statement of financial position date (rolling approach).

label.example: Unrecorded Unconditional Purchase Obligation, to be Paid, Year Four

description.example: Amount of fixed and determinable portion of unrecorded unconditional purchase obligation to be paid in fourth fiscal year following current fiscal year. Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported from current statement of financial position date (rolling approach).

label.example: Unrecorded Unconditional Purchase Obligation, to be Paid, Year Two

description.example: Amount of fixed and determinable portion of unrecorded unconditional purchase obligation to be paid in second fiscal year following current fiscal year. Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported from current statement of financial position date (rolling approach).

label.example: Unrecorded Unconditional Purchase Obligation, to be Paid, Year Three

description.example: Amount of fixed and determinable portion of unrecorded unconditional purchase obligation to be paid in third fiscal year following current fiscal year. Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported from current statement of financial position date (rolling approach).

label.example: Unrecorded Unconditional Purchase Obligation

description.example: Amount of the unrecorded obligation to transfer funds in the future for fixed or minimum amounts or quantities of goods or services at fixed or minimum prices (for example, as in take-or-pay contracts or throughput contracts).

label.example: Unrecorded Unconditional Purchase Obligation, to be Paid, after Year Five

description.example: Amount of fixed and determinable portion of unrecorded unconditional purchase obligation to be paid after fifth fiscal year following current fiscal year. Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported from current statement of financial position date (rolling approach).

label.example: Variable Lease, Cost

description.example: Amount of variable lease cost, excluded from lease liability, recognized when obligation for payment is incurred for finance and operating leases.

label.example: Weighted Average Number Diluted Shares Outstanding Adjustment

description.example: The sum of dilutive potential common shares or units used in the calculation of the diluted per-share or per-unit computation.

label.example: Weighted Average Number of Shares Outstanding, Diluted

description.example: The average number of shares or units issued and outstanding that are used in calculating diluted EPS or earnings per unit (EPU), determined based on the timing of issuance of shares or units in the period.

label.example: Weighted Average Number of Shares Outstanding, Basic

description.example: Number of [basic] shares or units, after adjustment for contingently issuable shares or units and other shares or units not deemed outstanding, determined by relating the portion of time within a reporting period that common shares or units have been outstanding to the total time in that period.

label.example: Deferred Tax Assets, Other Comprehensive Loss

description.example: Amount before allocation of valuation allowances of deferred tax asset attributable to deductible temporary differences from unrealized losses in other comprehensive income.

label.example: Debt Securities, Available-for-sale, Restricted

description.example: Amount of restricted investment in debt security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in other comprehensive income (available-for-sale).

label.example: Inventory, Work in Process and Raw Materials, Net of Reserves

description.example: The aggregate carrying amount, net of valuation reserves and adjustments, as of the balance sheet date of items which are partially completed at the time of measurement and unprocessed items that will go through the production process and become part of the final product. This element may be used when the reporting entity combines work in process and raw materials into an aggregate amount.

label.example: Unrecorded Unconditional Purchase Obligation, to be Paid, Remainder of Fiscal Year

description.example: Amount of fixed and determinable portion of unrecorded unconditional purchase obligation to be paid in remainder of current fiscal year.

label.example: Inventory, Raw Materials and Purchased Parts, Net of Reserves

description.example: Carrying amount, net of valuation reserves and adjustments, as of the balance sheet date of items purchased for use as components of a finished product or pieces of machinery and equipment plus any items in their natural and unrefined state. This element may be used when the reporting entity combines raw materials and purchased parts into an aggregate amount.

label.example: Hedged Asset, Fair Value Hedge

description.example: Amount of asset hedged in fair value hedging relationship.

label.example: Hedged Liability, Fair Value Hedge

description.example: Amount of liability hedged in fair value hedging relationship.

label.example: Deferred Income Tax Assets, Net

description.example: Amount, after allocation of valuation allowances and deferred tax liability, of deferred tax asset attributable to deductible differences and carryforwards, with jurisdictional netting.

label.example: Deferred Tax Liabilities, Property, Plant and Equipment

description.example: Amount of deferred tax liability attributable to taxable temporary differences from property, plant, and equipment.

label.example: Incremental Common Shares Attributable to Dilutive Effect of Share-based Payment Arrangements

description.example: Additional shares included in the calculation of diluted EPS as a result of the potentially dilutive effect of share based payment arrangements using the treasury stock method.

label.example: Other Assets, Miscellaneous, Noncurrent

description.example: Amount of other miscellaneous assets expected to be realized or consumed after one year or normal operating cycle, if longer.

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Cash Flow Hedge, Gain (Loss), after Reclassification and Tax

description.example: Amount, after tax and reclassification, of gain (loss) from derivative instrument designated and qualifying as cash flow hedge included in assessment of hedge effectiveness.

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Cash Flow Hedge, Gain (Loss), before Reclassification, after Tax

description.example: Amount, after tax and before reclassification, of gain (loss) from derivative instrument designated and qualifying as cash flow hedge included in assessment of hedge effectiveness.

label.example: General and Administrative Expense

description.example: The aggregate total of expenses of managing and administering the affairs of an entity, including affiliates of the reporting entity, which are not directly or indirectly associated with the manufacture, sale or creation of a product or product line.

label.example: Effective Income Tax Rate Reconciliation, Change in Deferred Tax Assets Valuation Allowance, Amount

description.example: Amount of the difference between reported income tax expense (benefit) and expected income tax expense (benefit) computed by applying the domestic federal statutory income tax rates to pretax income (loss) from continuing operations attributable to increase (decrease) in the valuation allowance for deferred tax assets.

label.example: Selling and Marketing Expense

description.example: The aggregate total amount of expenses directly related to the marketing or selling of products or services.

label.example: Share-based Payment Arrangement, Decrease for Tax Withholding Obligation

description.example: Amount of decrease to equity for grantee's tax withholding obligation for award under share-based payment arrangement.

label.example: Accounts Receivable, Allowance for Credit Loss

description.example: Amount of allowance for credit loss on accounts receivable.

label.example: Debt Securities, Available-for-Sale, Fair Value, Maturity, Allocated and Single Maturity Date, after Year 5 Through 10

description.example: Fair value of investment in debt security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in other comprehensive income (available-for-sale), with single maturity date and allocated without single maturity date, maturing in sixth through tenth fiscal year following current fiscal year. Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported from current statement of financial position date (rolling approach).

label.example: Debt Securities, Available-for-Sale, Fair Value, Maturity, Allocated and Single Maturity Date, after Year One Through Five

description.example: Fair value of investment in debt security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in other comprehensive income (available-for-sale), with single maturity date and allocated without single maturity date, maturing in second through fifth fiscal year following current fiscal year. Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported from current statement of financial position date (rolling approach).

label.example: Debt Securities, Available-for-Sale, Fair Value, Maturity, Allocated and Single Maturity Date, after Year 10

description.example: Fair value of investment in debt security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in other comprehensive income (available-for-sale), with single maturity date and allocated without single maturity date, maturing after tenth fiscal year following current fiscal year. Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported from current statement of financial position date (rolling approach).

label.example: Debt Securities, Available-for-Sale, Fair Value, Maturity, Allocated and Single Maturity Date, Year One
