description.example: Fair value of investment in debt security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in other comprehensive income (available-for-sale), with single maturity date and allocated without single maturity date, maturing in next fiscal year following current fiscal year. Excludes interim and annual periods when interim periods are reported from current statement of financial position date (rolling approach).

label.example: Available-for-sale Securities, Gross Realized Gain (Loss)

description.example: This item represents the difference between the gross realized gains and losses realized on the sale of debt or equity securities categorized neither as held-to-maturity nor trading securities. Additionally, this item would include any losses recognized for other than temporary impairments (OTTI) of the subject investments in debt and equity securities.

label.example: Available-for-sale Securities, Gross Realized Gains

description.example: This item represents the gross profit realized on the sale of debt or equity securities categorized neither as held-to-maturity nor trading securities.

label.example: Available-for-sale Securities, Gross Realized Losses

description.example: This item represents the gross loss realized on the sale of debt or equity securities categorized neither as held-to-maturity nor trading securities.

label.example: Business Combination, Step Acquisition, Equity Interest in Acquiree, Remeasurement Gain

description.example: In a business combination achieved in stages, this element represents the amount of gain recognized by the entity as a result of remeasuring to fair value the equity interest in the acquiree it held before the business combination.

label.example: Capital Leased Assets, Gross

description.example: Amount before accumulated depreciation of leased physical assets used in the normal conduct of business to produce goods and services.

label.example: Capital Lease Obligations, Current

description.example: Amount of capital lease obligation due within one year or the normal operating cycle, if longer.

label.example: Capital Lease Obligations, Noncurrent

description.example: Amount equal to the present value (the principal) at the beginning of the lease term of minimum lease payments during the lease term (excluding that portion of the payments representing executory costs such as insurance, maintenance, and taxes to be paid by the lessor, together with any profit thereon) net of payments or other amounts applied to the principal, through the balance sheet date and due to be paid more than one year (or one operating cycle, if longer) after the balance sheet date.

label.example: Cash and Cash Equivalents, at Carrying Value, Including Discontinued Operations

description.example: For the entity and the disposal group, cash includes currency on hand as well as demand deposits with banks or financial institutions. It also includes other kinds of accounts that have the general characteristics of demand deposits in that the Entity may deposit additional funds at any time and also effectively may withdraw funds at any time without prior notice or penalty. Cash equivalents, excluding items classified as marketable securities, include short-term, highly liquid investments that are both readily convertible to known amounts of cash, and so near their maturity that they present minimal risk of changes in value because of changes in interest rates. Generally, only investments with original maturities of three months or less qualify under that definition. Original maturity means original maturity to the entity holding the investment. For example, both a three-month US Treasury bill and a three-year Treasury note purchased three months from maturity qualify as cash equivalents. However, a Treasury note purchased three years ago does not become a cash equivalent when its remaining maturity is three months. Compensating balance arrangements that do not legally restrict the withdrawal or usage of cash amounts may be reported as Cash and Cash Equivalents, while legally restricted deposits held as compensating balances against borrowing arrangements, contracts entered into with others, or company statements of intention with regard to particular deposits are not generally reported as cash and cash equivalents.

label.example: Cash and Cash Equivalents, Fair Value Disclosure

description.example: Fair value portion of currency on hand as well as demand deposits with banks or financial institutions. Includes other kinds of accounts that have the general characteristics of demand deposits. Also includes short-term, highly liquid investments that are both readily convertible to known amounts of cash and so near their maturity that they present insignificant risk of changes in value because of changes in interest rates.

label.example: Cash, Cash Equivalents, and Short-term Investments

description.example: Cash includes currency on hand as well as demand deposits with banks or financial institutions. It also includes other kinds of accounts that have the general characteristics of demand deposits in that the customer may deposit additional funds at any time and effectively may withdraw funds at any time without prior notice or penalty. Cash equivalents, excluding items classified as marketable securities, include short-term, highly liquid Investments that are both readily convertible to known amounts of cash, and so near their maturity that they present minimal risk of changes in value because of changes in interest rates. Generally, only investments with original maturities of three months or less qualify under that definition. Original maturity means original maturity to the entity holding the investment. For example, both a three-month US Treasury bill and a three-year Treasury note purchased three months from maturity qualify as cash equivalents. However, a Treasury note purchased three years ago does not become a cash equivalent when its remaining maturity is three months. Short-term investments, exclusive of cash equivalents, generally consist of marketable securities intended to be sold within one year (or the normal operating cycle if longer) and may include trading securities, available-for-sale securities, or held-to-maturity securities (if maturing within one year), as applicable.

label.example: Common Stock, Capital Shares Reserved for Future Issuance

description.example: Aggregate number of common shares reserved for future issuance.

label.example: Contract with Customer, Liability, Noncurrent

description.example: Amount of obligation to transfer good or service to customer for which consideration has been received or is receivable, classified as noncurrent.

label.example: Convertible Preferred Stock, Nonredeemable or Redeemable, Issuer Option, Value

description.example: Value of outstanding nonredeemable convertible preferred stock or outstanding convertible preferred stock that is redeemable solely at the option of the issuer.

label.example: Cost Method Investments

description.example: Amount, after adjustment, of cost-method investment. Adjustments include, but are not limited to, dividends received in excess of earnings after date of investment that are considered a return of investment and other than temporary impairments.

label.example: Cost Method Investments, Fair Value Disclosure

description.example: Fair value portion of investments accounted under the cost method.

label.example: Cost of Revenue

description.example: The aggregate cost of goods produced and sold and services rendered during the reporting period.

label.example: Costs and Expenses

description.example: Total costs of sales and operating expenses for the period.

label.example: Cumulative Effect of New Accounting Principle in Period of Adoption (Deprecated 2020-01-31)

description.example: Amount after tax of increase (decrease) to equity or (increase) decrease to net assets, resulting from the cumulative effect adjustment of a new accounting principle applied in the period of adoption.

label.example: Current Federal, State and Local, Tax Expense (Benefit)

description.example: Amount of current state, local, and federal tax expense (benefit) pertaining to income (loss) from continuing operations.

label.example: Current Income Tax Expense (Benefit)

description.example: Amount of current income tax expense (benefit) pertaining to taxable income (loss) from continuing operations.

label.example: Customer Advances and Deposits

description.example: Refundable consideration, usually cash, held by the entity pending satisfactory completion of the entity's obligations or pending the closing of a contract.

label.example: Debt and Equity Securities, Gain (Loss)

description.example: Amount of unrealized and realized gain (loss) on investment in debt and equity securities.

label.example: Debt, Current

description.example: Amount of short-term debt and current maturity of long-term debt and capital lease obligations due within one year or the normal operating cycle, if longer.

label.example: Debt Instrument, Face Amount

description.example: Face (par) amount of debt instrument at time of issuance.

label.example: Debt Securities, Available-for-sale, Allowance for Credit Loss

description.example: Amount of allowance for credit loss on investment in debt security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in other comprehensive income (available-for-sale).

label.example: Debt Securities, Available-for-sale, Allowance for Credit Loss, Not Previously Recorded

description.example: Amount of expense for credit loss on investment in debt security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in other comprehensive income (available-for-sale), for which no credit loss was previously recorded.

label.example: Debt Securities, Available-for-sale, Allowance for Credit Loss, Recovery

description.example: Amount of increase in allowance for credit loss on investment in debt security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in other comprehensive income (available-for-sale), from recovery.

label.example: Debt Securities, Available-for-sale, Realized Gain

description.example: Amount of realized gain on investment in debt security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in other comprehensive income (available-for-sale).

label.example: Debt Securities, Available-for-sale, Realized Loss

description.example: Amount of realized loss on investment in debt security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in other comprehensive income (available-for-sale).

label.example: Debt Securities, Gain (Loss)

description.example: Amount of unrealized and realized gain (loss) on investment in debt security measured at amortized cost (held-to-maturity), investment in debt security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in other comprehensive income (available-for-sale) and investment in debt security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in net income (trading).

label.example: Debt Securities, Realized Gain (Loss)

description.example: Amount of realized gain (loss) on investment in debt security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in other comprehensive income (available-for-sale), investment in debt security measured at amortized cost (held-to-maturity) and investment in debt security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in net income (trading).

label.example: Deferred Federal, State and Local, Tax Expense (Benefit)

description.example: Amount of deferred state, local, and federal tax expense (benefit) pertaining to income (loss) from continuing operations.

label.example: Deferred Income Taxes and Tax Credits

description.example: Amount of deferred income tax expense (benefit) and income tax credits.

label.example: Deferred Revenue, Revenue Recognized

description.example: Amount of revenue recognized that was previously reported as deferred or unearned revenue.

label.example: Deferred Tax Assets, Capital Loss Carryforwards

description.example: Amount before allocation of valuation allowances of deferred tax asset attributable to deductible capital loss carryforwards.

label.example: Deferred Tax Assets, Net, Noncurrent

description.example: Amount, after allocation of valuation allowances and deferred tax liability, of deferred tax asset attributable to deductible differences and carryforwards, with jurisdictional netting and classified as noncurrent.

label.example: Deferred Tax Assets, Operating Loss Carryforwards

description.example: Amount before allocation of valuation allowances of deferred tax asset attributable to deductible operating loss carryforwards.

label.example: Deferred Tax Assets, State Taxes

description.example: Amount before allocation of valuation allowances of deferred tax asset attributable to deductible temporary differences from state taxes.

label.example: Deferred Tax Assets, Tax Credit Carryforwards, Other

description.example: Amount, before allocation of valuation allowance, of deferred tax asset attributable to deductible tax credit carryforwards, classified as other.

label.example: Deferred Tax Assets, Tax Deferred Expense, Compensation and Benefits, Employee Benefits

description.example: Amount, before allocation of valuation allowance, of deferred tax asset attributable to deductible temporary differences from employee benefits, classified as other.

label.example: Deferred Tax Assets, Tax Deferred Expense, Reserves and Accruals, Legal Settlements

description.example: Amount before allocation of valuation allowances of deferred tax asset attributable to deductible temporary differences from the estimated loss from legal settlements.

label.example: Deferred Tax Assets, Tax Deferred Expense, Reserves and Accruals, Other

description.example: Amount, before allocation of valuation allowance, of deferred tax asset attributable to deductible temporary differences from reserves and accruals, classified as other.

label.example: Deferred Tax Liabilities, Intangible Assets

description.example: Amount of deferred tax liability attributable to taxable temporary differences from intangible assets other than goodwill.

label.example: Deferred Tax Liabilities, Investments

description.example: Amount of deferred tax liability attributable to taxable temporary differences from investments (excludes investments in subsidiaries and equity method investments).

label.example: Deposits Received for Securities Loaned, at Carrying Value

description.example: The amount of cash received as security in return for loaning securities to another party.

label.example: Derivative, Amount of Hedged Item

description.example: Amount of hedged item in hedging relationship. Excludes hedged asset or liability.

label.example: Derivative Asset, Fair Value, Gross Liability

description.example: Fair value of liability associated with financial asset or other contract with one or more underlyings, notional amount or payment provision or both, and the contract can be net settled by means outside the contract or delivery of an asset.

label.example: Derivative Asset, Fair Value, Offset Against Collateral, Net of Not Subject to Master Netting Arrangement, Policy Election

description.example: Fair value, after effects of master netting arrangements, deduction of assets not subject to a master netting arrangement and elected not to be offset, of financial asset or other contract with one or more underlyings, notional amount or payment provision or both, and the contract can be net settled by means outside the contract or delivery of an asset, offset against an obligation to return collateral.

label.example: Derivative Asset, Not Offset, Policy Election Deduction

description.example: Fair value of financial asset or other contract with one or more underlyings, notional amount or payment provision or both, and the contract can be net settled by means outside the contract or delivery of an asset, elected not to be offset, deducted from derivative assets.

label.example: Derivative Asset, Securities Purchased under Agreements to Resell, Securities Borrowed

description.example: Amount, after the effects of master netting arrangements, of derivative assets, securities purchased under agreements to resell and securities borrowed. Includes assets not subject to a master netting arrangement and not elected to be offset.

label.example: Derivative Asset, Securities Purchased under Agreements to Resell, Securities Borrowed, Amount Offset Against Collateral

description.example: Amount, after effects of master netting arrangements, of derivative assets, securities purchased under agreements to resell and securities borrowed offset against an obligation to return collateral under a master netting arrangement. Includes assets not subject to a master netting arrangement and not elected to be offset.

label.example: Derivative Asset, Securities Purchased under Agreements to Resell, Securities Borrowed, Collateral, Obligation to Return Cash

description.example: Amount of obligation to return cash collateral under master netting arrangements that have not been offset against derivative assets, securities purchased under agreements to resell and securities borrowed.

label.example: Derivative Asset, Securities Purchased under Agreements to Resell, Securities Borrowed, Collateral, Obligation to Return Securities

description.example: Amount of obligation to return securities collateral under master netting arrangements that have not been offset against derivative assets, securities purchased under agreements to resell and securities borrowed.

label.example: Derivative Asset, Securities Purchased under Agreements to Resell, Securities Borrowed, Gross

description.example: Amount, before effects of master netting arrangements, of derivative assets, securities purchased under agreements to resell and securities borrowed. Includes assets elected not to be offset. Excludes assets not subject to a master netting arrangement.

label.example: Derivative Asset, Securities Purchased under Agreements to Resell, Securities Borrowed, Liability

description.example: Amount of liability associated with derivative assets, securities purchased under agreements to resell and securities borrowed.

label.example: Derivative Asset, Securities Purchased under Agreements to Resell, Securities Borrowed, Not Offset, Policy Election Deduction

description.example: Amount of derivative assets, securities purchased under agreements to resell and securities borrowed, elected not to be offset, deducted from derivative assets, securities purchased under agreements to resell and securities borrowed.

label.example: Derivative Asset, Securities Purchased under Agreements to Resell, Securities Borrowed, Offset Against Collateral, Net of Not Subject to Master Netting Arrangement, Policy Election

description.example: Amount, after effects of master netting arrangements, deduction of assets not subject to a master netting arrangement and elected not to be offset, of derivative assets, securities purchased under agreements to resell and securities borrowed, offset against an obligation to return collateral under a master netting arrangement.

label.example: Derivative Asset

description.example: Fair value, after the effects of master netting arrangements, of a financial asset or other contract with one or more underlyings, notional amount or payment provision or both, and the contract can be net settled by means outside the contract or delivery of an asset. Includes assets not subject to a master netting arrangement and not elected to be offset.

label.example: Derivative, Collateral, Obligation to Return Securities

description.example: Amount of obligation to return securities collateral under master netting arrangements that have not been offset against derivative assets.

label.example: Derivative, Collateral, Right to Reclaim Cash

description.example: Amount of right to receive cash collateral under master netting arrangements that have not been offset against derivative liabilities.

label.example: Derivative, Collateral, Right to Reclaim Securities

description.example: Amount of right to receive securities collateral under master netting arrangements that have not been offset against derivative liabilities.

label.example: Derivative Asset, Fair Value, Gross Asset

description.example: Fair value, before effects of master netting arrangements, of a financial asset or other contract with one or more underlyings, notional amount or payment provision or both, and the contract can be net settled by means outside the contract or delivery of an asset. Includes assets elected not to be offset. Excludes assets not subject to a master netting arrangement.

label.example: Derivative Asset, Fair Value, Amount Offset Against Collateral

description.example: Fair value, after effects of master netting arrangements, of financial asset or other contract with one or more underlyings, notional amount or payment provision or both, and the contract can be net settled by means outside the contract or delivery of an asset offset against an obligation to return collateral. Includes assets not subject to a master netting arrangement and not elected to be offset.

label.example: Derivative Liability, Fair Value, Gross Liability

description.example: Fair value, before effects of master netting arrangements, of a financial liability or contract with one or more underlyings, notional amount or payment provision or both, and the contract can be net settled by means outside the contract or delivery of an asset. Includes liabilities elected not to be offset. Excludes liabilities not subject to a master netting arrangement.

label.example: Derivative Liability, Fair Value, Amount Offset Against Collateral

description.example: Fair value, after effects of master netting arrangements, of financial liability or contract with one or more underlyings, notional amount or payment provision or both, and the contract can be net settled by means outside the contract or delivery of an asset offset against the right to receive collateral. Includes liabilities not subject to a master netting arrangement and not elected to be offset.

label.example: Derivative Liability

description.example: Fair value, after the effects of master netting arrangements, of a financial liability or contract with one or more underlyings, notional amount or payment provision or both, and the contract can be net settled by means outside the contract or delivery of an asset. Includes liabilities not subject to a master netting arrangement and not elected to be offset.

label.example: Derivative Liability, Fair Value, Gross Asset

description.example: Fair value of asset associated with financial liability or contract with one or more underlyings, notional amount or payment provision or both, and the contract can be net settled by means outside the contract or delivery of an asset.

label.example: Derivative Liability, Fair Value, Offset Against Collateral, Net of Not Subject to Master Netting Arrangement, Policy Election

description.example: Fair value, after effects of master netting arrangements, deduction of liabilities not subject to a master netting arrangement and elected not to be offset, of financial liability or contract with one or more underlyings, notional amount or payment provision or both, and the contract can be net settled by means outside the contract or delivery of an asset, offset against the right to receive collateral.

label.example: Derivative Liability, Not Offset, Policy Election Deduction

description.example: Fair value of financial liability or contract with one or more underlyings, notional amount or payment provision or both, and the contract can be net settled by means outside the contract or delivery of an asset, elected not to be offset, deducted from derivative liabilities.

label.example: Derivative Liability, Securities Sold under Agreements to Repurchase, Securities Loaned

description.example: Amount, after the effects of master netting arrangements, of derivative liabilities, securities sold under agreements to repurchase and securities loaned. Includes liabilities not subject to a master netting arrangement and not elected to be offset.

label.example: Derivative Liability, Securities Sold under Agreements to Repurchase, Securities Loaned, Amount Offset Against Collateral

description.example: Amount, after effects of master netting arrangements, of derivative liabilities, securities sold under agreements to repurchase and securities loaned offset against a right to receive collateral. Includes liabilities not subject to a master netting arrangement and not elected to be offset.

label.example: Derivative Liability, Securities Sold under Agreements to Repurchase, Securities Loaned, Asset

description.example: Amount of asset associated with derivative liabilities, securities sold under agreements to repurchase and securities loaned.

label.example: Derivative Liability, Securities Sold under Agreements to Repurchase, Securities Loaned, Collateral, Right to Reclaim Cash

description.example: Amount of right to receive cash collateral under master netting arrangements that have not been offset against derivative liabilities, securities sold under agreements to repurchase and securities loaned.

label.example: Derivative Liability, Securities Sold under Agreements to Repurchase, Securities Loaned, Collateral, Right to Reclaim Securities

description.example: Amount of right to receive securities collateral under master netting arrangements that have not been offset against derivative liabilities, securities sold under agreements to repurchase and securities loaned.

label.example: Derivative Liability, Securities Sold under Agreements to Repurchase, Securities Loaned, Gross

description.example: Amount, before effects of master netting arrangements, of derivative liabilities, securities sold under agreements to repurchase and securities loaned. Includes liabilities elected not to be offset. Excludes liabilities not subject to a master netting arrangement.

label.example: Derivative Liability, Securities Sold under Agreements to Repurchase, Securities Loaned, Not Offset, Policy Election Deduction

description.example: Amount of derivative liabilities, securities sold under agreements to repurchase and securities loaned, elected not to be offset, deducted from derivative liabilities, securities sold under agreements to repurchase and securities loaned.

label.example: Derivative Liability, Securities Sold under Agreements to Repurchase, Securities Loaned, Offset Against Collateral, Net of Not Subject to Master Netting Arrangement, Policy Election

description.example: Amount, after effects of master netting arrangements, deduction of liabilities not subject to a master netting arrangement and elected not to be offset, of derivative liabilities, securities sold under agreements to repurchase and securities loaned, offset against a right to receive collateral.

label.example: Disposal Group, Including Discontinued Operation, Cash and Cash Equivalents

description.example: Amount classified as cash and cash equivalents attributable to disposal group held for sale or disposed of.

label.example: Effective Income Tax Rate Reconciliation, Change in Deferred Tax Assets Valuation Allowance, Percent

description.example: Percentage of the difference between reported income tax expense (benefit) and expected income tax expense (benefit) computed by applying the domestic federal statutory income tax rates to pretax income (loss) from continuing operations attributable to changes in the valuation allowance for deferred tax assets.

label.example: Effective Income Tax Rate Reconciliation, Change in Enacted Tax Rate, Percent

description.example: Percentage of the difference between reported income tax expense (benefit) and expected income tax expense (benefit) computed by applying the domestic federal statutory income tax rates to pretax income (loss) from continuing operations attributable to changes in the income tax rates.

label.example: Effective Income Tax Rate Reconciliation, Nondeductible Expense, Share-based Payment Arrangement, Percent

description.example: Percentage of difference between reported income tax expense (benefit) and expected income tax expense (benefit) computed by applying domestic federal statutory income tax rate to pretax income (loss) from continuing operation, attributable to nondeductible expense for share-based payment arrangement.

label.example: Effective Income Tax Rate Reconciliation, Other Adjustments, Percent

description.example: Percentage of the difference between reported income tax expense (benefit) and expected income tax expense (benefit) computed by applying the domestic federal statutory income tax rates to pretax income (loss) from continuing operations attributable to other adjustments.

label.example: Effective Income Tax Rate Reconciliation,Other Reconciling Items, Percent

description.example: Percentage of the difference, between reported income tax expense (benefit) and the expected income tax expense (benefit) computed by applying the domestic federal statutory income tax rates to pretax income (loss) from continuing operations, that is attributable to tax exempt income, equity in earnings (loss) of an unconsolidated subsidiary, minority interest income (expense), tax holiday, disposition of a business, disposition of an asset, repatriation of foreign earnings, repatriation of foreign earnings jobs creation act of 2004, change in enacted tax rate, prior year income taxes, change in deferred tax asset valuation allowance, and other adjustments.

label.example: Effective Income Tax Rate Reconciliation, State and Local Income Taxes, Percent

description.example: Percentage of the difference between reported income tax expense (benefit) and expected income tax expense (benefit) computed by applying the domestic federal statutory income tax rates to pretax income (loss) from continuing operations applicable to state and local income tax expense (benefit), net of federal tax expense (benefit).

label.example: Effective Income Tax Rate Reconciliation, Tax Credit, Percent

description.example: Percentage of the difference between reported income tax expense (benefit) and expected income tax expense (benefit) computed by applying the domestic federal statutory income tax rates to pretax income (loss) from continuing operations attributable to tax credits. Including, but not limited to, research credit, foreign tax credit, investment tax credit, and other tax credits.

label.example: Effective Income Tax Rate Reconciliation, Tax Credit, Research, Percent

description.example: Percentage of the difference between reported income tax expense (benefit) and expected income tax expense (benefit) computed by applying the domestic federal statutory income tax rates to pretax income (loss) from continuing operations attributable to research tax credit.

label.example: Effective Income Tax Rate Reconciliation, Tax Cuts and Jobs Act, Percent

description.example: Percentage of reported income tax expense (benefit) in excess of (less than) expected income tax expense (benefit) computed by applying domestic federal statutory income tax rate to pretax income (loss) from continuing operations, attributable to Tax Cuts and Jobs Act.

label.example: Effective Income Tax Rate Reconciliation, Tax Cuts and Jobs Act, Transition Tax on Accumulated Foreign Earnings, Percent

description.example: Percentage of reported income tax expense (benefit) in excess of (less than) expected income tax expense (benefit) computed by applying domestic federal statutory income tax rate to pretax income (loss) from continuing operations, attributable to increase (decrease) from transition tax on accumulated earnings of controlled foreign corporation deemed repatriated pursuant to Tax Cuts and Jobs Act.

label.example: Effect of Exchange Rate on Cash and Cash Equivalents

description.example: Amount of increase (decrease) from the effect of exchange rate changes on cash and cash equivalent balances held in foreign currencies.

label.example: Effect of Exchange Rate on Cash, Cash Equivalents, Restricted Cash and Restricted Cash Equivalents

description.example: Amount of increase (decrease) from effect of exchange rate changes on cash and cash equivalents, and cash and cash equivalents restricted to withdrawal or usage; held in foreign currencies. Excludes amounts for disposal group and discontinued operations. Cash includes, but is not limited to, currency on hand, demand deposits with banks or financial institutions, and other accounts with general characteristics of demand deposits. Cash equivalents include, but are not limited to, short-term, highly liquid investments that are both readily convertible to known amounts of cash and so near their maturity that they present insignificant risk of changes in value because of changes in interest rates.

label.example: Equity Method Investments

description.example: This item represents the carrying amount on the entity's balance sheet of its investment in common stock of an equity method investee. This is not an indicator of the fair value of the investment, rather it is the initial cost adjusted for the entity's share of earnings and losses of the investee, adjusted for any distributions (dividends) and other than temporary impairment (OTTI) losses recognized.

label.example: Equity Securities, FV-NI

description.example: Amount of investment in equity security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in net income (FV-NI).

label.example: Equity Securities, FV-NI and without Readily Determinable Fair Value

description.example: Amount of investment in equity security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in net income (FV-NI) and investment in equity security without readily determinable fair value.

label.example: Equity Securities, FV-NI, Cost

description.example: Cost of investment in equity security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in net income (FV-NI). Excludes equity method investment and investment in equity security without readily determinable fair value.

label.example: Equity Securities, FV-NI, Gain (Loss)

description.example: Amount of unrealized and realized gain (loss) on investment in equity security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in net income (FV-NI).

label.example: Equity Securities, FV-NI, Realized Gain (Loss)

description.example: Amount of realized gain (loss) from sale of investment in equity security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in net income (FV-NI).

label.example: Equity Securities, FV-NI, Unrealized Gain (Loss)

description.example: Amount of unrealized gain (loss) on investment in equity security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in net income (FV-NI).

label.example: Equity Securities, FV-NI, Unrealized Loss

description.example: Amount of unrealized loss on investment in equity security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in net income (FV-NI).

label.example: Equity Securities without Readily Determinable Fair Value, Downward Price Adjustment, Annual Amount

description.example: Amount of loss from downward price adjustment on investment in equity security without readily determinable fair value.

label.example: Equity Securities without Readily Determinable Fair Value, Downward Price Adjustment, Cumulative Amount

description.example: Amount of cumulative loss from downward price adjustment on investment in equity security without readily determinable fair value.

label.example: Equity Securities without Readily Determinable Fair Value, Upward Price Adjustment, Annual Amount

description.example: Amount of gain from upward price adjustment on investment in equity security without readily determinable fair value.

label.example: Equity Securities without Readily Determinable Fair Value, Upward Price Adjustment, Cumulative Amount

description.example: Amount of cumulative gain from upward price adjustment on investment in equity security without readily determinable fair value.

label.example: Excess Tax Benefit from Share-based Compensation, Operating Activities

description.example: Amount of cash outflow for realized tax benefit related to deductible compensation cost reported on the entity's tax return for equity instruments in excess of the compensation cost for those instruments recognized for financial reporting purposes.

label.example: Finite-Lived Intangible Asset, Expected Amortization, Remainder of Fiscal Year

description.example: Amount of amortization for assets, excluding financial assets and goodwill, lacking physical substance with finite life expected to be recognized in remainder of current fiscal year.

label.example: Foreign Currency Cash Flow Hedge Gain (Loss) to be Reclassified During Next 12 Months

description.example: The estimated net amount of unrealized gains or losses on foreign currency cash flow hedges at the reporting date expected to be reclassified to earnings within the next 12 months.

label.example: Foreign Currency Transaction Gain (Loss), before Tax

description.example: Amount before tax of foreign currency transaction realized and unrealized gain (loss) recognized in the income statement.

label.example: Foreign Currency Transaction Loss, before Tax

description.example: Amount before tax of foreign currency transaction realized and unrealized loss recognized in the income statement.

label.example: Gain (Loss) on Hedging Activity

description.example: Gain (loss) incurred in hedging activities.

label.example: Gain (Loss) on Disposition of Business

description.example: Amount of gain (loss) from sale and disposal of integrated set of activities and assets capable of being conducted and managed for purpose of providing return in form of dividend, lower cost, or other economic benefit to investor, owner, member and participant.

label.example: Gains (Losses) on Restructuring of Debt

description.example: For a debtor, the aggregate gain (loss) recognized on the restructuring of payables arises from the difference between the book value of the debt before the restructuring and the fair value of the payments on the debt after restructuring is complete.

label.example: Goodwill, Other Changes (Deprecated 2016-01-31)

description.example: Amount of other (increase) decrease of an asset representing the future economic benefits arising from other assets acquired in a business combination that are not individually identified and separately recognized.

label.example: Goodwill, Transfers

description.example: Amount of transfers into (out of) an asset representing the future economic benefits arising from other assets acquired in a business combination that are not individually identified and separately recognized.

label.example: Goodwill, Translation and Purchase Accounting Adjustments

description.example: Amount of increase (decrease) from foreign currency translation adjustments and purchase accounting adjustments of an asset representing future economic benefits arising from other assets acquired in a business combination that are not individually identified and separately recognized.

label.example: Goodwill, Written off Related to Sale of Business Unit

description.example: Amount of divestiture of an asset representing future economic benefits arising from other assets acquired in a business combination that are not individually identified and separately recognized.

label.example: Other than Temporary Impairment Losses, Investments

description.example: The amount by which the fair value of an investment is less than the amortized cost basis or carrying amount of that investment at the balance sheet date and the decline in fair value is deemed to be other than temporary, before considering whether or not such amount is recognized in earnings or other comprehensive income.

label.example: Income (Loss) from Continuing Operations, Net of Tax, Attributable to Parent

description.example: Amount after tax of income (loss) from continuing operations attributable to the parent.

label.example: Income (Loss) from Continuing Operations before Income Taxes, Domestic

description.example: The portion of earnings or loss from continuing operations before income taxes that is attributable to domestic operations.

label.example: Income (Loss) from Continuing Operations, Per Basic Share

description.example: The amount of net income (loss) from continuing operations per each share of common stock or unit outstanding during the reporting period.

label.example: Income (Loss) from Continuing Operations, Per Diluted Share

description.example: The amount of net income (loss) derived from continuing operations during the period available to each share of common stock or common unit outstanding during the reporting period and to each share or unit that would have been outstanding assuming the issuance of common shares or units for all dilutive potential common shares or units outstanding during the reporting period.

label.example: Income (Loss) from Discontinued Operations, Net of Tax, Including Portion Attributable to Noncontrolling Interest

description.example: Amount after tax of income (loss) from a discontinued operation including the portion attributable to the noncontrolling interest. Includes, but is not limited to, the income (loss) from operations during the phase-out period, gain (loss) on disposal, gain (loss) for reversal of write-down (write-down) to fair value, less cost to sell, and adjustments to a prior period gain (loss) on disposal.

label.example: Income (Loss) from Equity Method Investments

description.example: Amount of income (loss) for proportionate share of equity method investee's income (loss).

label.example: Income Taxes Paid

description.example: The amount of cash paid during the current period to foreign, federal, state, and local authorities as taxes on income.

label.example: Income Taxes Receivable, Current

description.example: Carrying amount due within one year of the balance sheet date (or one operating cycle, if longer) from tax authorities as of the balance sheet date representing refunds of overpayments or recoveries based on agreed-upon resolutions of disputes.

label.example: Increase (Decrease) in Accrued Liabilities

description.example: The increase (decrease) during the reporting period in the aggregate amount of expenses incurred but not yet paid.

label.example: Increase (Decrease) in Collateral Held under Securities Lending

description.example: The increase (decrease) during the period in the carrying amount of assets, which may include but are not limited to cash and investments, received as collateral pertaining to securities loaned to a counterparty to the transaction. Includes the change during the period in any unrealized gain or loss on such investments.

label.example: Increase (Decrease) in Income Taxes

description.example: The increase (decrease) during the reporting period in the amounts payable to taxing authorities for taxes that are based on the reporting entity's earnings, net of amounts receivable from taxing authorities for refunds of overpayments or recoveries of income taxes, and in deferred and other tax liabilities and assets.

label.example: Interest Costs Capitalized

description.example: Amount of interest capitalized during the period.

label.example: Interest Income, Other

description.example: Amount of interest income earned from interest bearing assets classified as other.

label.example: Lease, Cost

description.example: Amount of lease cost recognized by lessee for lease contract.

label.example: Letters of Credit Outstanding, Amount

description.example: The total amount of the contingent obligation under letters of credit outstanding as of the reporting date.

label.example: Liability for Uncertainty in Income Taxes, Noncurrent

description.example: Amount recognized for uncertainty in income taxes classified as noncurrent.

label.example: Line of Credit Facility, Maximum Borrowing Capacity

description.example: Maximum borrowing capacity under the credit facility without consideration of any current restrictions on the amount that could be borrowed or the amounts currently outstanding under the facility.

label.example: Long-term Debt and Lease Obligation

description.example: Amount of long-term debt and lease obligation, classified as noncurrent.

label.example: Long-term Debt and Lease Obligation, Current

description.example: Amount of long-term debt and lease obligation, classified as current.

label.example: Long-term Debt and Lease Obligation, Including Current Maturities

description.example: Amount of long-term debt and lease obligation, including portion classified as current.

label.example: Long-term Debt, Fair Value

description.example: The fair value amount of long-term debt whether such amount is presented as a separate caption or as a parenthetical disclosure. Additionally, this element may be used in connection with the fair value disclosures required in the footnote disclosures to the financial statements. The element may be used in both the balance sheet and disclosure in the same submission.

label.example: Loss Contingency, Accrual, Current

description.example: Amount of loss contingency liability expected to be resolved within one year or the normal operating cycle, if longer.

label.example: Loss Contingency, Loss in Period

description.example: The amount of loss pertaining to the specified contingency that was charged against earnings in the period, including the effects of revisions in previously reported estimates.

label.example: Marketable Securities

description.example: Amount of investment in marketable security.

label.example: Marketing and Advertising Expense

description.example: The total expense recognized in the period for promotion, public relations, and brand or product advertising.

label.example: Net Income (Loss) Available to Common Stockholders, Basic

description.example: Amount, after deduction of tax, noncontrolling interests, dividends on preferred stock and participating securities; of income (loss) available to common shareholders.

label.example: Noncontrolling Interest, Increase from Sale of Parent Equity Interest

description.example: Amount of increase in noncontrolling interest from sale of a portion of the parent's controlling interest.

label.example: Noncontrolling Interest in Variable Interest Entity

description.example: Portion of equity (net assets) in a variable interest entity (VIE) not attributable, directly or indirectly, to the parent entity. That is, this is the portion of equity in a VIE that is attributable to the noncontrolling interest (previously referred to as minority interest).

label.example: OCI, before Reclassifications, Net of Tax, Attributable to Parent

description.example: Amount after tax, before reclassification adjustments, of other comprehensive income (loss), attributable to parent.

label.example: Operating Leases, Future Minimum Payments Receivable

description.example: Future minimum rental payments in aggregate as of the balance sheet date under operating leases.

label.example: Operating Leases, Future Minimum Payments Receivable, Current

description.example: Future rental payments receivable within one year of the balance sheet date under an operating lease.

label.example: Operating Leases, Future Minimum Payments Receivable, in Five Years

description.example: Future rental payments receivable within the fifth year from the balance sheet date under an operating lease.

label.example: Operating Leases, Future Minimum Payments Receivable, in Four Years

description.example: Future rental payments receivable within the fourth year from the balance sheet date under an operating lease.

label.example: Operating Leases, Future Minimum Payments Receivable, in Three Years

description.example: Future rental payments receivable within the third year from the balance sheet date under an operating lease.

label.example: Operating Leases, Future Minimum Payments Receivable, in Two Years

description.example: Future rental payments receivable within the second year from the balance sheet date under an operating lease.

label.example: Operating Leases, Future Minimum Payments Receivable, Thereafter

description.example: Future minimum lease payments receivable under operating leases for periods greater than five years following the balance sheet date.

label.example: Operating Lease, Weighted Average Discount Rate, Percent

description.example: Weighted average discount rate for operating lease calculated at point in time.

label.example: OCI, Debt Securities, Available-for-Sale, Gain (Loss), after Adjustment, Tax

description.example: Amount, after adjustment, of tax expense (benefit) for unrealized gain (loss) on investment in debt security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in other comprehensive income (available-for-sale) and tax expense (benefit) for unrealized gain (loss) on investment in debt security measured at amortized cost (held-to-maturity) from transfer to available-for-sale.

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Cash Flow Hedge, Gain (Loss), after Reclassification, Tax

description.example: Amount, after reclassification, of tax expense (benefit) for gain (loss) from derivative instrument designated and qualifying as cash flow hedge included in assessment of hedge effectiveness.

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), Derivatives Qualifying as Hedges, Tax

description.example: Amount of tax expense (benefit), after reclassification adjustments, of increase (decrease) in accumulated gain (loss) from derivative instruments designated and qualifying as the effective portion of cash flow hedges and an entity's share of an equity investee's increase (decrease) in deferred hedging gain (loss).

label.example: Other Long-term Investments

description.example: Amount of long-term investments classified as other.

label.example: Other than Temporary Impairment Losses, Investments, Available-for-sale Securities

description.example: The amount by which the fair value of an investment in debt and equity securities categorized as Available-for-sale is less than the amortized cost basis or carrying amount of that investment at the balance sheet date and the decline in fair value is deemed to be other than temporary, before considering whether or not such amount is recognized in earnings or other comprehensive income.

label.example: Payments to Acquire Buildings

description.example: The cash outflow for acquisition of buildings (properties) whether for investment or use.

label.example: Payments to Acquire Interest in Subsidiaries and Affiliates

description.example: The cash outflow associated with the acquisition of or advances to an entity that is related to it but not strictly controlled (for example, an unconsolidated subsidiary, affiliate, and joint venture or equity method investment) or the acquisition of an additional interest in a subsidiary (controlled entity).

label.example: Payments to Acquire Marketable Securities

description.example: Amount of cash outflow for purchase of marketable security.

label.example: Preferred Stock Dividends and Other Adjustments

description.example: The aggregate value of preferred stock dividends and other adjustments necessary to derive net income apportioned to common stockholders.

label.example: Preferred Stock, Par or Stated Value Per Share

description.example: Face amount or stated value per share of preferred stock nonredeemable or redeemable solely at the option of the issuer.

label.example: Preferred Stock, Shares Issued

description.example: Total number of nonredeemable preferred shares (or preferred stock redeemable solely at the option of the issuer) issued to shareholders (includes related preferred shares that were issued, repurchased, and remain in the treasury). May be all or portion of the number of preferred shares authorized. Excludes preferred shares that are classified as debt.

label.example: Preferred Stock, Shares Outstanding

description.example: Aggregate share number for all nonredeemable preferred stock (or preferred stock redeemable solely at the option of the issuer) held by stockholders. Does not include preferred shares that have been repurchased.

label.example: Proceeds from Collection of Notes Receivable

description.example: The cash inflow associated with principal collections from a borrowing supported by a written promise to pay an obligation.

label.example: Proceeds from Debt, Net of Issuance Costs

description.example: The cash inflow from additional borrowings, net of cash paid to third parties in connection with debt origination.

label.example: Proceeds from Divestiture of Businesses

description.example: The cash inflow associated with the amount received from the sale of a portion of the company's business, for example a segment, division, branch or other business, during the period.

label.example: Proceeds from Noncontrolling Interests

description.example: Amount of cash inflow from a noncontrolling interest. Includes, but is not limited to, purchase of additional shares or other increase in noncontrolling interest ownership.

label.example: Proceeds from (Payments for) Securities Purchased under Agreements to Resell

description.example: The net cash flow from purchases followed by sales of securities under terms of reverse repurchase agreements.

label.example: Proceeds from Sale and Maturity of Marketable Securities

description.example: The cash inflow associated with the aggregate amount received by the entity through sale or maturity of marketable securities (held-to-maturity or available-for-sale) during the period.

label.example: Proceeds from Sale of Property, Plant, and Equipment

description.example: The cash inflow from the sale of long-lived, physical assets that are used in the normal conduct of business to produce goods and services and not intended for resale.

label.example: Proceeds from Stock Options Exercised

description.example: Amount of cash inflow from exercise of option under share-based payment arrangement.

label.example: Property, Plant, and Equipment and Finance Lease Right-of-Use Asset, Accumulated Depreciation and Amortization

description.example: Amount of accumulated depreciation and amortization from plant, property, and equipment and right-of-use asset from finance lease.

label.example: Property, Plant, and Equipment and Finance Lease Right-of-Use Asset, after Accumulated Depreciation and Amortization

description.example: Amount, after accumulated depreciation and amortization, of property, plant, and equipment and finance lease right-of-use asset.

label.example: Property, Plant, and Equipment and Finance Lease Right-of-Use Asset, before Accumulated Depreciation and Amortization

description.example: Amount, before accumulated depreciation and amortization, of property, plant, and equipment and finance lease right-of-use asset.

label.example: Purchase Obligation

description.example: Minimum amount of purchase arrangement in which the entity has agreed to expend funds to procure goods or services from a supplier.

label.example: Reclassification from AOCI, Current Period, Net of Tax, Attributable to Parent

description.example: Amount after tax of reclassification adjustments of other comprehensive income (loss) attributable to parent.

label.example: Repayments of Debt

description.example: The cash outflow during the period from the repayment of aggregate short-term and long-term debt. Excludes payment of capital lease obligations.

label.example: Repayments of Debt and Lease Obligation

description.example: Amount of cash outflow for short-term and long-term debt and lease obligation.

label.example: Revenue, Remaining Performance Obligation, Amount

description.example: Amount of transaction price allocated to performance obligation that has not been recognized as revenue.

label.example: Right-of-Use Asset Obtained in Exchange for Operating Lease Liability

description.example: Amount of increase in right-of-use asset obtained in exchange for operating lease liability.

label.example: Secured Borrowings, Gross, Difference, Amount

description.example: Amount of secured borrowings in excess of (less than) the liability for repurchase agreements and securities lending transactions, before offset, included in offsetting disclosures.

label.example: Securities Loaned

description.example: Amount, after the effects of master netting arrangements, of securities loaned to entities in exchange for collateral. Includes liabilities not subject to a master netting arrangement and not elected to be offset.

label.example: Securities Loaned, Amount Offset Against Collateral

description.example: Amount, after effects of master netting arrangements, of securities loaned to entities in exchange for collateral offset against a right to receive collateral. Includes liabilities not subject to a master netting arrangement and not elected to be offset.

label.example: Securities Loaned, Asset

description.example: Amount of asset associated with securities loaned to entities in exchange for collateral.

label.example: Securities Loaned, Collateral, Right to Reclaim Cash

description.example: Amount of right to receive cash collateral under master netting arrangements that have not been offset against securities loaned.

label.example: Securities Loaned, Collateral, Right to Reclaim Securities

description.example: Amount of right to receive securities collateral under master netting arrangements that have not been offset against securities loaned.

label.example: Securities Loaned, Gross

description.example: Amount, before effects of master netting arrangements, of securities loaned to entities in exchange for collateral. Includes liabilities elected not to be offset. Excludes liabilities not subject to a master netting arrangement.

label.example: Securities Loaned, Including Not Subject to Master Netting Arrangement and Assets other than Securities Transferred

description.example: Amount, before effects of master netting arrangements, of financial assets loaned to entities in exchange for collateral. Includes liabilities not subject to a master netting arrangement and financial assets transferred other than securities that qualify as secured borrowings.

label.example: Securities Loaned, Not Offset, Policy Election Deduction

description.example: Amount of securities loaned to entities in exchange for collateral, elected not to be offset, deducted from securities loaned.

label.example: Securities Loaned, Offset Against Collateral, Net of Not Subject to Master Netting Arrangement, Policy Election

description.example: Amount, after effects of master netting arrangements, deduction of liabilities not subject to a master netting arrangement and elected not to be offset, of securities loaned to entities in exchange for collateral, offset against a right to receive collateral.

label.example: Securities Owned Not Readily Marketable (Deprecated 2019-01-31)

description.example: Aggregate carrying value of owned private equity or other securities for which a quoted market price is not readily determinable, the value of which has been determined by management based upon fair value methods, including pricing of similar securities and valuation techniques and is included in the schedule of financial condition or the balance sheet.

label.example: Securities Purchased under Agreements to Resell

description.example: Amount, after the effects of master netting arrangements, of funds outstanding loaned in the form of a security resale agreement between the entity and another party for the purchase and resale of identical or substantially the same securities at a date certain for a specified price. Includes purchases of participations in pools of securities that are subject to a resale agreement, assets not subject to a master netting arrangement and not elected to be offset.

label.example: Securities Purchased under Agreements to Resell, Amount Offset Against Collateral

description.example: Amount, after effects of master netting arrangements, of funds outstanding loaned in the form of a security resale agreement between the entity and another party for the purchase and resale of identical or substantially the same securities at a date certain for a specified price offset against an obligation to return collateral. Includes assets not subject to a master netting arrangement and not elected to be offset.

label.example: Securities Purchased under Agreements to Resell, Collateral, Obligation to Return Cash

description.example: Amount of obligation to return cash collateral under master netting arrangements that have not been offset against securities purchased under agreement to resell.

label.example: Securities Purchased under Agreements to Resell, Collateral, Obligation to Return Securities

description.example: Amount of obligation to return securities collateral under master netting arrangements that have not been offset against securities purchased under agreement to resell.

label.example: Securities Purchased under Agreements to Resell, Gross

description.example: Amount, before effects of master netting arrangements, of funds outstanding loaned in the form of a security resale agreement between the entity and another party for the purchase and resale of identical or substantially the same securities at a date certain for a specified price. Includes assets elected not to be offset. Excludes assets not subject to a master netting arrangement.

label.example: Securities Purchased under Agreements to Resell, Liability

description.example: Amount of liability associated with funds outstanding loaned in the form of a security resale agreement between the entity and another party for the purchase and resale of identical or substantially the same securities at a date certain for a specified price.

label.example: Securities Purchased under Agreements to Resell, Not Offset, Policy Election Deduction

description.example: Amount of funds outstanding loaned in the form of a security resale agreement between the entity and another party for the purchase and resale of identical or substantially the same securities at a date certain for a specified price, elected not to be offset, deducted from securities purchased under agreements to resell.

label.example: Securities Purchased under Agreements to Resell, Offset Against Collateral, Net of Not Subject to Master Netting Arrangement, Policy Election

description.example: Amount, after effects of master netting arrangements, deduction of assets not subject to a master netting arrangement and elected not to be offset, of funds outstanding loaned in the form of a security resale agreement between the entity and another party for the purchase and resale of identical or substantially the same securities at a date certain for a specified price, offset against an obligation to return collateral.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Equity Instruments Other than Options, Share-based Liabilities Paid

description.example: Amount of cash paid to settle liability for award under share-based payment arrangement.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Forfeitures and Expirations in Period

description.example: For presentations that combine terminations, the number of shares under options that were cancelled during the reporting period as a result of occurrence of a terminating event specified in contractual agreements pertaining to the stock option plan or that expired.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Vested and Expected to Vest, Exercisable, Aggregate Intrinsic Value

description.example: Amount by which current fair value of underlying stock exceeds exercise price of fully vested and expected to vest exercisable or convertible options. Includes, but is not limited to, unvested options for which requisite service period has not been rendered but that are expected to vest based on achievement of performance condition, if forfeitures are recognized when they occur.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Vested and Expected to Vest, Exercisable, Number

description.example: Number of fully vested and expected to vest exercisable options that may be converted into shares under option plan. Includes, but is not limited to, unvested options for which requisite service period has not been rendered but that are expected to vest based on achievement of performance condition, if forfeitures are recognized when they occur.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Vested and Expected to Vest, Exercisable, Weighted Average Exercise Price

description.example: Weighted-average exercise price, at which grantee can acquire shares reserved for issuance, for fully vested and expected to vest exercisable or convertible options. Includes, but is not limited to, unvested options for which requisite service period has not been rendered but that are expected to vest based on achievement of performance condition, if forfeitures are recognized when they occur.

label.example: Share-based Compensation Arrangement by Share-based Payment Award, Options, Vested in Period, Fair Value

description.example: Fair value of options vested. Excludes equity instruments other than options, for example, but not limited to, share units, stock appreciation rights, restricted stock.

label.example: Stock Issued

description.example: The fair value of stock issued in noncash financing activities.

label.example: Stock Issued During Period, Value, New Issues

description.example: Equity impact of the value of new stock issued during the period. Includes shares issued in an initial public offering or a secondary public offering.

label.example: Tax Credit Carryforward, Amount

description.example: The amount of the tax credit carryforward, before tax effects, available to reduce future taxable income under enacted tax laws.

label.example: Tax Cuts and Jobs Act, Incomplete Accounting, Change in Tax Rate, Deferred Tax Asset, Provisional Income Tax Expense (Deprecated 2020-01-31)

description.example: Amount of reasonable estimate for income tax expense for which accounting for tax effect for remeasurement of deferred tax asset from change in tax rate is incomplete pursuant to Tax Cuts and Jobs Act.

label.example: Tax Cuts and Jobs Act, Incomplete Accounting, Provisional Income Tax Expense (Benefit) (Deprecated 2020-01-31)

description.example: Amount of reasonable estimate for income tax expense (benefit) for which accounting for tax effect is incomplete pursuant to Tax Cuts and Jobs Act.

label.example: Variable Interest Entity, Consolidated, Assets, Pledged (Deprecated 2020-01-31)

description.example: Amount of consolidated variable interest entity's (VIE) assets that can only be used to settle obligations of VIE.

label.example: Variable Interest Entity, Consolidated, Liabilities, No Recourse (Deprecated 2020-01-31)

description.example: Amount of consolidated variable interest entity's (VIE) liabilities that do not have recourse to general credit of primary beneficiary.

label.example: Variable Interest Entity, Reporting Entity Involvement, Maximum Loss Exposure, Amount

description.example: The reporting entity's maximum amount of exposure to loss as a result of its involvement with the Variable Interest Entity (VIE).

label.example: Business Combination, Recognized Identifiable Assets Acquired and Liabilities Assumed, Finite-Lived Intangibles

description.example: The amount of identifiable intangible assets recognized as of the acquisition date.

label.example: Debt Securities, Available-for-sale, Allowance for Credit Loss, Period Increase (Decrease)

description.example: Amount of increase (decrease) in allowance for credit loss of investment in debt security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in other comprehensive income (available-for-sale).

label.example: Debt Securities, Available-for-sale, Gain (Loss)

description.example: Amount of unrealized and realized gain (loss) on investment in debt security measured at fair value with change in fair value recognized in other comprehensive income (available-for-sale).

label.example: Deferred Tax Assets, in Process Research and Development

description.example: Amount before allocation of valuation allowances of deferred tax asset attributable to deductible temporary differences from in-process research and development costs expensed in connection with a business combination.

label.example: Restructuring Charges

description.example: Amount of expenses associated with exit or disposal activities pursuant to an authorized plan. Excludes expenses related to a discontinued operation or an asset retirement obligation.

label.example: Stock Issued During Period, Shares, New Issues

description.example: Number of new stock issued during the period.

label.example: Distributed Earnings

description.example: The total amount of dividends declared in the period for each class of stock and the contractual amount of dividends (or interest on participating income bonds) that must be paid for the period (for example, unpaid cumulative dividends). Dividends declared in the current period do not include dividends declared in respect of prior-period unpaid cumulative dividends. Preferred dividends that are cumulative only if earned are deducted only to the extent that they are earned.

label.example: Net Income (Loss) Available to Common Stockholders, Diluted

description.example: Amount, after deduction of tax, noncontrolling interests, dividends on preferred stock and participating securities, and addition from assumption of issuance of common shares for dilutive potential common shares; of income (loss) available to common shareholders.

label.example: Undistributed Earnings (Loss) Available to Common Shareholders, Basic

description.example: Amount of undistributed earnings (loss) allocated to common stock as if earnings had been distributed. Excludes distributed earnings.

label.example: Adjustments to Additional Paid in Capital, Dividends in Excess of Retained Earnings

description.example: Amount of decrease in additional paid in capital (APIC) resulting from dividends legally declared (or paid) in excess of retained earnings balance.

label.example: Loss Contingency Accrual, Payments

description.example: Amount of cash outflow reducing loss contingency liability.

label.example: Construction in Progress, Gross

description.example: Amount of structure or a modification to a structure under construction. Includes recently completed structures or modifications to structures that have not been placed into service.

label.example: Finance Lease, Interest Expense

description.example: Amount of interest expense on finance lease liability.

label.example: Finance Lease, Interest Payment on Liability

description.example: Amount of interest paid on finance lease liability.

label.example: Finance Lease, Principal Payments

description.example: Amount of cash outflow for principal payment on finance lease.

label.example: Finance Lease, Right-of-Use Asset, Accumulated Amortization

description.example: Amount of accumulated amortization of right-of-use asset from finance lease.

label.example: Finance Lease, Right-of-Use Asset, Amortization

description.example: Amount of amortization expense attributable to right-of-use asset from finance lease.

label.example: Finance Lease, Right-of-Use Asset, before Accumulated Amortization

description.example: Amount, before accumulated amortization, of right-of-use asset from finance lease.

label.example: Finance Lease, Weighted Average Discount Rate, Percent

description.example: Weighted average discount rate for finance lease calculated at point in time.

label.example: Right-of-Use Asset Obtained in Exchange for Finance Lease Liability

description.example: Amount of increase in right-of-use asset obtained in exchange for finance lease liability.

label.example: Commissions Payable to Broker-Dealers and Clearing Organizations

description.example: Amount payable to broker-dealers or clearing organizations for executing or clearing trades or orders.

label.example: Revenue Not from Contract with Customer

description.example: Amount of revenue that is not accounted for under Topic 606.

label.example: Effective Income Tax Rate Reconciliation, Nondeductible Expense, Other, Percent

description.example: Percentage of the difference between reported income tax expense (benefit) and expected income tax expense (benefit) computed by applying the domestic federal statutory income tax rates to pretax income (loss) from continuing operations attributable to other nondeductible expenses.

label.example: Effective Income Tax Rate Reconciliation, Tax Expense (Benefit), Share-based Payment Arrangement, Percent

description.example: Percentage of difference between reported income tax expense (benefit) and expected income tax expense (benefit) computed by applying domestic federal statutory income tax rate to pretax income (loss) from continuing operation, attributable to expense for award under share-based payment arrangement. Excludes expense determined to be nondeductible upon grant or after for award under share-based payment arrangement.

label.example: Effective Income Tax Rate Reconciliation, Tax Contingency, Percent

description.example: Percentage of the difference between reported income tax expense (benefit) and expected income tax expense (benefit) computed by applying the domestic federal statutory income tax rates to pretax income (loss) from continuing operations attributable to income tax contingencies. Includes, but not limited to, domestic tax contingency, foreign tax contingency, state and local tax contingency, and other contingencies.

label.example: Effective Income Tax Rate Reconciliation, Tax Credit, Foreign, Percent

description.example: Percentage of the difference between reported income tax expense (benefit) and expected income tax expense (benefit) computed by applying the domestic federal statutory income tax rates to pretax income (loss) from continuing operations attributable to foreign tax credit.

label.example: Effective Income Tax Rate Reconciliation, Tax Credit, Other, Percent

description.example: Percentage of the difference between reported income tax expense (benefit) and expected income tax expense (benefit) computed by applying the domestic federal statutory income tax rates to pretax income (loss) from continuing operations attributable to other tax credits.

label.example: Guarantor Obligations, Maximum Exposure, Undiscounted

description.example: Maximum potential amount of future payments (undiscounted) the guarantor could be required to make under the guarantee or each group of similar guarantees before reduction for potential recoveries under recourse or collateralization provisions.

label.example: Effective Income Tax Rate Reconciliation, Change in Enacted Tax Rate, Amount

description.example: Amount of the difference between reported income tax expense (benefit) and expected income tax expense (benefit) computed by applying the domestic federal statutory income tax rates to pretax income (loss) from continuing operations, attributable to increase (decrease) in the income tax rates.

label.example: Effective Income Tax Rate Reconciliation, Nondeductible Expense, Other, Amount

description.example: Amount of the difference between reported income tax expense (benefit) and expected income tax expense (benefit) computed by applying the domestic federal statutory income tax rates to pretax income (loss) from continuing operations attributable to other nondeductible expenses.

label.example: Effective Income Tax Rate Reconciliation, Tax Contingency, Amount

description.example: Amount of the difference between reported income tax expense (benefit) and expected income tax expense (benefit) computed by applying the domestic federal statutory income tax rates to pretax income (loss) from continuing operations attributable to increase (decrease) in income tax contingencies. Including, but not limited to, domestic tax contingency, foreign tax contingency, state and local tax contingency, and other contingencies.

label.example: Effective Income Tax Rate Reconciliation, Tax Credit, Foreign, Amount

description.example: Amount of the difference between reported income tax expense (benefit) and expected income tax expense (benefit) computed by applying the domestic federal statutory income tax rates to pretax income (loss) from continuing operations attributable to foreign tax credit.

label.example: Effective Income Tax Rate Reconciliation, Tax Credit, Other, Amount

description.example: Amount of the difference between reported income tax expense (benefit) and expected income tax expense (benefit) computed by applying the domestic federal statutory income tax rates to pretax income (loss) from continuing operations attributable to other tax credits.

label.example: Other Comprehensive Income (Loss), before Tax, Portion Attributable to Parent

description.example: Amount before tax of other comprehensive income (loss) attributable to parent entity.
